The attacker has disadvantage on the attack roll. If the protected creature hasn’t attacked or harmed another creature within the last minute, the attacker’s attack misses instead. Crimson Jelly As light probes the darkness, it strikes a tiny oval-shaped bubble. Within its translucent form, strings of undulating comb-like structures scintillate with an eerie crimson glow.

reaction.name: Translucent Terrors
entries: Crimson jellies drift upon currents of endless darkness in deep waters or the underworld. These aggressive carnivores feed upon whatever creatures they come across. Their translucent forms are nearly invisible, and they move so rapidly, few creatures realize their presence until a jelly begins feasting on them.

reaction.name: Swarming
entries: Crimson jellies reproduce asexually and with extreme speed. Almost immediately after feeding, the creatures break away from prey and divide. Within moments of encountering a single jelly, the unwary can be overwhelmed by a growing mob of ravenous jellies.

reaction.name: Elder Siphons
entries: Void scholars theorize that an ancient evil god or void lord created crimson jellies to collect knowledge by extracting it from the minds of sentient beings. A creature knocked unconscious by a crimson jelly temporarily loses its memories, recalling only the vaguest memory of an unpronounceable name and the partial image of a crimson rune.

monster.name: Crimson Jelly
senses: keensense 10 ft. (can’t sense beyond this radius)

trait.name: Amorphous
entries: The crimson jelly can move through space as narrow as 1 inch wide without squeezing.

trait.name: Blood Sense
entries: The crimson jelly can pinpoint, by scent, the location of creatures that aren’t Constructs or Undead and that don’t have all of their HP within 60 feet of it and can sense the general direction of such creatures within 1 mile of it.

trait.name: Ooze Nature
entries: The crimson jelly doesn’t require sleep.

trait.name: Ooze Resilience
entries: The crimson jelly is resistant to the restrained condition, and it is immune to exhaustion and to the blinded, charmed, deafened, frightened, and prone conditions.

trait.name: Tainted Attacks
entries: A creature that is reduced to 0 HP by a crimson jelly must succeed on a DC 9 CHA save or suffer one level of exhaustion. While a creature suffers from this exhaustion, it loses most of its memories aside from basic information about itself, such as its name and its capabilities, and it is wracked with nightmarish visions that include a crimson rune.

trait.name: Transparent
entries: While in an area of dim or bright light, the crimson jelly is invisible. While in darkness, creatures without darkvision can see the jelly’s faint crimson glow.

action.name: Feeding Paddles
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) necrotic damage. The crimson jelly gains temporary HP equal to the necrotic damage dealt, and it attaches to the target. If the jelly had advantage on this attack, it attaches to the target’s face, leaving the target unable to breathe or speak while the jelly is attached. While attached, the crimson jelly can use only the Feeding Paddles action, and it moves with the target whenever the target moves, requiring none of the jelly’s movement. The Crimson jelly can detach itself by spending 5 feet of its movement. A creature, including the target, can take its action to detach the jelly by succeeding on a DC 12 STR check.

bonus.name: Reproduce (Requires Temporary HP)
entries: While the crimson jelly has 1 or more temporary HP , it can split part of itself off into a new crimson jelly with HP equal to the original crimson jelly’s temporary HP . The original crimson jelly then loses any temporary HP it has. The new crimson jelly otherwise has all the same statistics as its parent, except the new jelly can’t gain temporary HP from Feeding Paddles attacks until it finishes a long rest. Darkmantle The stalactite loosens itself from its mooring and spreads out a fleshy membrane in anticipation of engulfing prey.

bonus.name: Instinctive Hunters
entries: Though less cunning than most of the underworld’s ambushers, darkmantles can prove dangerous to unwary travelers. When they aren’t digesting meals, they rest in cavernous ceilings and floors, blending in with stalactites and stalagmites in their hunting grounds. A darkmantle’s primary method of attack involves enveloping its victim’s head and then either crushing or suffocating the victim. While holding onto a victim, a darkmantle can whirl its body to throw off the victim’s balance, forcing them both to the ground where the darkmantle can devour the helpless victim.

bonus.name: Unnatural Shroud
entries: If the darkmantle’s prey travels in a group or might be saved by its fellows, the darkmantle cloaks itself in supernatural darkness, rendering itself and its victim invisible to most surface dwellers.

bonus.name: Aboveground Sightings
entries: Darkmantles facing competition from other darkmantles or more powerful predators leave their underworld homes and alight in stony ruins where their disguises won’t stand out. They find plenty of potential meals from treasure hunters, wandering merchants, and creatures seeking shelter from inclement weather.

trait.name: Echolocation
entries: The darkmantle can’t use its keensense while deafened.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the darkmantle remains motionless, it is indistinguishable from a cave formation, such as a stalactite or stalagmite.

trait.name: Jagged Hide
entries: A creature that hits the darkmantle with a melee attack while within 5 feet of it takes 2 (1d4) piercing damage.

trait.name: Monstrosity Resilience
entries: The darkmantle is resistant to exhaustion and to the frightened condition.

action.name: Crush
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) bludgeoning damage, and the darkmantle attaches to it. If the target is Medium or smaller and the darkmantle has advantage on the attack roll, the darkmantle attaches to the target’s head, and the target is blinded and unable to breathe while the darkmantle is attached. While attached, the darkmantle can’t make Crush attacks, and at the start of each of the darkmantle’s turns, the target takes 6 (1d6 + 3) bludgeoning damage. The attached darkmantle moves with the target whenever the target moves, requiring none of the darkmantle’s movement. It can detach itself by spending 5 feet of its movement on its turn. A creature, including the target, can take its action to detach the darkmantle by succeeding on a DC 13 STR check.

action.name: Topple
entries: While attached to a creature, the darkmantle swings its body to throw off the balance of the creature to which it is attached. The attached target must succeed on a DC 13 DEX save or be knocked prone. If the target fails the save and is already prone, it is restrained while the darkmantle is attached to it, as the darkmantle pins it to the ground.

bonus.name: Darkness Aura (1/Day)
entries: A 15-foot radius of magical darkness extends out from the darkmantle, moves with it, and spreads around corners. The darkness lasts as long as the darkmantle maintains concentration, up to 10 minutes (as if concentrating on a spell). Darkvision can’t penetrate this darkness, and no natural light can illuminate it. If any of the darkness overlaps with an area of light created by a spell of 2nd level or lower, the spell creating the light is dispelled. Death Dog This cruel, brutish canine bears two heads, drool dripping from each of its sharp-toothed jaws. A carrion reek surrounds it, and its four eyes burn with hunger.

bonus.name: Murderous Hounds
entries: Two-headed and full of hate, death dogs delight in killing and devouring any humanoids they can find. Unlike other dogs, they don’t form large packs, though they will join with one or two others of their kind to bring down more powerful prey.

bonus.name: A Singular Pack
entries: One of the death dog’s heads is always in command and makes decisions, while the other head follows that head’s lead. When hunting or defending itself, the death dog’s heads act in unison. This arrangement usually lasts for a death dog’s whole life, but in rare cases, the non-dominant head will challenge the dominant head for leadership, resulting in the death dog fighting itself.

bonus.name: Plague Dogs
entries: The mouths of death dogs carry disease, and those who succumb often watch in horror as their flesh turns necrotic and begins to rot. While death dogs are almost impossible to train, bandits and other marauders sometimes capture them and release them near unsuspecting settlements. Even if the dogs are brought down by the settlement’s defenders, those bitten often succumb to the disease soon afterward, weakening the settlement’s resistance to other attackers.

trait.name: Monstrosity Resilience
entries: The death dog is resistant to exhaustion and to the frightened condition.

trait.name: Multiple Heads
entries: The death dog has two heads and is resistant to the blinded, charmed, deafened, frightened, stunned, and unconscious conditions.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The death knight makes three Greatsword or Necrotic Bolt attacks.

action.name: Greatsword
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) slashing damage and 7 (2d6) necrotic damage.

action.name: Necrotic Bolt
entries: Ranged Spell Attack: +7 to hit, range 60 ft., one target. Hit: 18 (4d6 + 4) necrotic damage.

bonus.name: Text
entries: Champion’s Challenge. The death knight challenges one creature it can see within 30 feet of it. The target must succeed on a DC 15 CHA save or have disadvantage on attack rolls against creatures that aren’t the knight until the end of its next turn.

reaction.name: Parry
entries: The death knight adds 3 to its AC against one melee attack that would hit it. To do so, the death knight must see the attacker and be wielding a melee weapon.

reaction.name: Soul Drain
entries: When the death knight reduces a Humanoid to 0 HP , it can absorb the creature’s soul. The target must succeed on a DC 15 WIS save or immediately die, its soul becoming trapped in the death knight’s sword. The death knight has advantage on attack rolls for 1 minute after absorbing a soul. The creature can’t be restored to life until the death knight is permanently destroyed. Death Knight A figure bedecked in dark, ornate armor styled in centuries-old fashion stands at the ready. Its massive sword features equally intricate designs.

reaction.name: Righteous Combatants
entries: Unliving knights who died dishonorably, death knights return to reclaim their honor in a twisted mockery of dueling. They believe in fair play in combat and always challenge the most competent warrior, but they have no qualms about using their magical abilities to dispatch ignoble foes. If a knight feels it has defeated a worthy opponent, it absorbs the opponent’s essence into itself. By absorbing the souls of honorable opponents, death knights hope to attain eternal rest.

reaction.name: Keeping Up Appearances
entries: Many death knights carry on with façades of their former lives, attending galas, participating in tournaments, and engaging in revelries. This lasts until people start to notice the death knight is oddly absent from meals or never drinks like it did before it died. Many who notice play along to avoid provoking a knight’s ire.

reaction.name: Soul Restoration
entries: Denied an afterlife because of their own hubris, death knights can’t be permanently destroyed until redeemed. If the knight can honorably repeat the dishonorable duel that led to its death or perform some great act of heroism, it can be permanently destroyed, freeing the knight’s soul from its endless unlife and also freeing every soul the knight had absorbed.

trait.name: Disgraced Rejuvenation
entries: Unless redeemed, a destroyed death knight returns in 1d4 days, regaining all its HP and becoming active again within 10 feet of where it died.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The death knight has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Undead Nature
entries: The death knight doesn’t require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Undead Resilience
entries: The death knight is immune to poison damage, to exhaustion, and to the poisoned condition. Deathless A gaunt, deathly pale man steps forward. A malicious grin splits his face as he holds up a porcelain nesting doll that bears a striking resemblance to him.

trait.name: Deathly Fey
entries: Some mortals of fey ancestry, such as elves, seek immortality through unnatural rituals. Similar to becoming a lich, this is a personal journey for each individual, but becoming a deathless is merely a step on that journey rather than the destination. The transformation into a deathless requires the fey magic in the person’s blood and drains much of the vitality out of their body, giving them a deathly appearance and temporarily halting aging.

trait.name: Soul Thief
entries: After its transformation, a deathless has exactly one year to find and bind a remarkable mortal soul. If it fails, its body crumbles to dust, and the deathless is permanently destroyed. However, if the deathless successfully binds a mortal soul five times—at least one each year—its former appearance is restored, and it becomes immortal.

trait.name: Remarkable Souls
entries: Deathless need to collect remarkable souls to complete their ascension, but the souls need not be moral. Because of this, many deathless ally with night hags and other fiends to gain choice souls from them. Night hags enjoy such bargains, as deathless that ascend to full, immortal fey can become powerful—and indebted—future allies for the hags.

trait.name: Dark Effigies
entries: A deathless carves wood or sculpts porcelain into a set of five nesting dolls in its former likeness, each of which can hold one soul. As long as at least one doll holding a soul is intact, a deathless can’t be killed, except by failing to continue to collect souls each year.

monster.name: Deathless
resistNoteFromTraits: bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from

trait.name: Fey Resilience
entries: The deathless is resistant to the charmed and unconscious conditions.

trait.name: Regeneration
entries: The deathless regains 10 HP at the start of its turn if it has at least 1 HP and at least 1 of its nesting dolls is intact.

trait.name: Soul Bound
entries: If it has at least 1 intact nesting doll with a bound soul, a destroyed deathless gains a new body in 5 days, regaining all its HP and becoming active again. The new body appears within 5 feet of the nesting doll (if multiple dolls contain souls, the deathless chooses which).

trait.name: Soul Stealing
entries: If the deathless reduces a creature to 0 HP with Death Blow, Death Bolt, or Call of Death, the target’s soul is transported into one of the deathless’s nesting dolls. A creature with a soul trapped in this way can’t be restored to life until all the deathless’s nesting dolls are destroyed.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The deathless makes three Death Blow or Death Bolt attacks.

action.name: Death Blow
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (2d8 + 4) bludgeoning damage plus 7 (2d6) necrotic damage. The target must succeed on a DC 16 CON save or its HP maximum is reduced by an amount equal to the necrotic damage taken. This reduction lasts until the target finishes a short or long rest. The target dies if this effect reduces its HP maximum to 0.

action.name: Death Bolt
entries: Ranged Spell Attack: +7 to hit, range 60 ft., one target. Hit: 20 (5d6 + 3) necrotic damage.

bonus.name: Call of Death
entries: One creature that doesn’t have all its HP and that the deathless can see within 30 feet must make a DC 16 CON save, taking 7 (2d6) necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Demon, Balor This fiery demon swings a sword with one hand and cracks a whip with the other. Its massive wings carry it just above the ground.

bonus.name: Demon Commander
entries: Balors use their might to command hordes of demons in battle. When balors point at targets, their followers rush to kill and rend with particular fear and energy. When a balor see their demonic troops running away from battle or leaving gaps for their enemies, it sometimes steps forward to turn the tide. Balors promise terrible torments to deserters and remind their subordinates that destruction in battle is better than slow death afterwards. Rumors claim balors devour deserters and traitors bodily, adding each victim’s power to their own.

bonus.name: Fiery Warriors
entries: Balors enjoy bloodying their weapons and are fearsome foes in battle. They dive out of the sky and bring their flaming weapons down onto unsuspecting foes. The fire that coats their bodies burns extremely hot, even scorching opponents ordinarily accustomed to heat and flames.

monster.name: Balor
resistNoteFromTraits: bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from

trait.name: Demonic Flames
entries: Creatures resistant to fire damage aren’t resistant to fire damage dealt by the balor. Creatures immune to fire damage are still immune to the balor’s fire damage.

trait.name: Demonic Resilience
entries: The balor is resistant to cold and lightning damage. In addition, it is immune to poison damage and to the poisoned condition.

trait.name: Diving Assault
entries: If the balor is flying and moves at least 30 feet straight toward a creature and then hits it with a Gore attack on the same turn, that target must succeed on a DC 20 STR save or be knocked prone. If the target is prone, the balor can make one Longsword attack against it as a bonus action.

trait.name: Flaming Weapons
entries: The balor’s weapon attacks are magical. When the balor hits with any weapon, the weapon deals an extra 3d8 fire damage (included in the attack).

trait.name: Fire Aura
entries: At the start of each of the balor’s turns, each creature within 10 feet of it takes 13 (3d8) fire damage, and flammable objects in the aura that aren’t being worn or carried ignite.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The balor has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The balor makes one Gore attack, one Longsword attack, and one Whip attack.

action.name: Gore
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +14 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 15 (2d6 + 8) piercing damage plus 13 (3d8) fire damage.

action.name: Longsword
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +14 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 21 (3d8 + 8) slashing damage, or 24 (3d10 + 8) slashing damage if used with two hands, plus 13 (3d8) fire damage. If the balor scores a critical hit, it rolls damage dice three times, instead of twice.

action.name: Whip
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +14 to hit, reach 30 ft., one target. Hit: 15 (3d4 + 8) slashing damage plus 13 (3d8) fire damage, and the target must succeed on a DC 20 Strength saving throw or be pulled up to 25 feet toward the balor.

bonus.name: Teleport
entries: The balor magically teleports, along with any equipment it is wearing or carrying, up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space it can see. Demon, Dretch Loose skin hangs on this bloated creature which waddles on short legs.

bonus.name: Least Demon
entries: The least of demons, dretches are the initial form of all demons and corrupted souls that start their existence in the Abyss. Some are promoted and transformed into stronger demons shortly after starting life in the Abyss, while others spend their entire existence as dretches. This transformation is sometimes triggered by higher-order demons and demon lords, though other dretches are transformed randomly by the enigmatic and chaotic energy of the Abyss.

bonus.name: Abyssal Rebirth
entries: Dretches are so tied to the Abyss that killing them in the Abyss doesn’t destroy them like it does most outsiders. Instead, dretches are reborn anew in the Abyss, providing constant fodder for demonic forces.

bonus.name: Gas-Filled
entries: Imbued with the poison of the Abyss, dretches can belch out a noxious cloud that sickens all who smell it. Militant demons often send waves of dretches out in front of their main forces to weaken their enemies with this gas, making their foes easy prey.

trait.name: Abyssal Rejuvenation
entries: Unlike other outsiders, a dretch that dies in its home plane of the Abyss is restored in a new body in 1d10 days, regaining all its HP and becoming active again. The new body appears in a random location in the Abyss. Only sprinkling holy water on the dretch’s remains can prevent this trait from functioning.

trait.name: Demonic Resilience
entries: The dretch is resistant to cold, fire, and lightning damage. In addition, it is immune to poison damage and to the poisoned condition.

action.name: Claws
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) slashing damage plus 2 (1d4) poison damage.

bonus.name: Fetid Cloud (1/Day)
entries: A 10-foot radius cloud of disgusting green gas extends out from the dretch, moves with it, and spreads around corners. The cloud’s area is lightly obscured and lasts 1 minute or until a strong wind disperses it. A creature that starts its turn in the cloud must succeed on a DC 11 CON save or be poisoned until the start of its next turn. While poisoned in this way, a creature can take either an action or a bonus action on its turn, not both, and can’t take reactions. Demon, Glabrezu This bestial demon has a canine head and feet, furry body, and two pairs of limbs, the upper pair ending in crushing pincers and the lower pair in gigantic clawed hands.

bonus.name: Deceptive Appearance
entries: Glabrezus’ bestial appearance belies their cunning nature. They enjoy the slow corruption of mortals, promising power in exchange for ever-increasing acts of depravity. If a mortal realizes what is happening and attempts to back out, the demon threatens to expose the mortal’s acts to its allies if it no longer complies. This moment of defeated acquiescence is ambrosia to the demon.

bonus.name: Masters of Fire and Lightning
entries: Though a glabrezu enjoys manipulation and wars of words, it is a devastating force on a battlefield. A glabrezu’s pincers grab and hold victims, crushing them into pulp, while the demon’s clawed hands create bolts of crackling fire and lightning to hurl into distant foes.

monster.name: Glabrezu
resistNoteFromTraits: bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from

trait.name: Demonic Resilience
entries: The glabrezu is resistant to cold, fire, and lightning damage. In addition, it is immune to poison damage and to the poisoned condition.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The glabrezu has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Point Blank Grappler
entries: When the glabrezu makes a Crackling Flame Bolt attack, it doesn’t have disadvantage on the attack roll from being within 5 feet of a hostile creature, provided it is grappling that creature.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The glabrezu makes two Claw attacks and two Pincer attacks, or it makes four Crackling Flame Bolt attacks. It can replace both Claw attacks with two Crackling Flame Bolt attacks.

action.name: Claw
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 10 (2d4 + 5) slashing damage plus 3 (1d6) fire damage and 3 (1d6) lightning damage.

action.name: Pincer
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 16 (2d10 + 5) bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it is grappled (escape DC 17). The glabrezu has two pincers, each of which can grapple only one target.

action.name: Crackling Flame Bolt
entries: Ranged Spell Attack: +7 to hit, range 60 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) fire damage plus 10 (3d6) lightning damage.

bonus.name: Manipulating Whispers
entries: The glabrezu whispers in the mind of a creature it can see within 120 feet of it, encouraging the creature to commit wicked acts. The target must succeed on a DC 17 CHA save or succumb to one of the following urges:
• Boastful Stance. The target is overcome with surety of its
own battle prowess, momentarily letting down its guard. Creatures have advantage on attack rolls against the target until the start of the glabrezu’s next turn.
• Confirmation of Wealth. The target is briefly overcome
with the desire to confirm the amount and status of its valuables. It uses either an action or bonus action on its next turn (the glabrezu’s choice) to count and take stock of its valuables.
• Wrathful Strike. Anger fills the target, and it immediately

lashes out at those nearby. The target uses its reaction to make one melee weapon attack against a creature of the glabrezu’s choice within 5 feet of the target. Demon, Golmana The demon’s teeth gnash hungrily as it moves from walking on four limbs to standing upright. It unfolds a second pair of smaller arms from its chest, its green skin glistening in the firelight. As its eyeless head turns toward the light, it releases a throaty growl.

bonus.name: Void-Touched Fiends
entries: Golmana haunt the darkest pits of the Abyss, where the boundaries between the Lower Planes and the Void are thin. Twisted by its contact with the Void, a golmana is filled with a hollow pain that recedes slightly each time it consumes a mortal’s body or soul.

bonus.name: Hunters in the Dark
entries: Golmana have exceptional hearing and don’t rely on light to hunt, making them particularly dangerous in dark caverns and alleyways. They climb along walls and ceilings, ambushing and devouring any creature they encounter. When closing on prey, golmana gnash their teeth repeatedly and screech a cacophony of Void Speech, disorienting victims with energies of the Void.

monster.name: Golmana
senses: keensense 60 ft. (can’t sense beyond this radius)

trait.name: Demonic Resilience
entries: The golmana is resistant to cold, fire, and lightning damage. In addition, it is immune to poison damage and to the poisoned condition.

trait.name: Heightened Hearing
entries: The golmana’s Perception is 19 when perceiving by hearing.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The golmana has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Spider Climb
entries: The golmana can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The golmana makes two Claws attacks and one Tail attack. If both Claws attacks hit a Medium or smaller creature, the target is grappled (escape DC 15). Until this grapple ends, the target is restrained, and the golmana can’t make Claws attacks against other targets.

action.name: Claws
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 17 (3d8 + 4) slashing damage.

action.name: Tail
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (2d8 + 4) piercing damage.

action.name: Void Screech (Recharge 5–6)
entries: The golmana lets out a keening screech in a 30-foot cone. Each creature that isn’t a Fiend in that area must make a DC 15 CON save. On a failure, a creature takes 14 (4d6) necrotic damage and 18 (4d8) thunder damage, and it has disadvantage on the next attack roll it makes before the start of the golmana’s next turn, as brief contact with the energies of the Void destabilizes the creature. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and doesn’t suffer this disadvantage on its next attack roll.

bonus.name: Consuming Rend
entries: The golmana violently digs its small, torso claws into one creature it is grappling. The target must make a DC 15 DEX save, taking 7 (2d6) slashing damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. The golmana then regains HP equal to the damage dealt Demon, Hezrou A hunched, warty fiend shaped like a bipedal frog opens its mouth to reveal rows of sharp teeth.

bonus.name: Swamp Dwellers
entries: Hezrous inhabit the poisonous swamps and bogs of the Abyss, and they enjoy wallowing in similar locations when on the Material Plane. T o hezrous, the smellier the swamp or bog the better.

bonus.name: Frog Friend
entries: Though demons have little regard for mortal life, hezrous feel a mild kinship with frogs and toads and rarely if ever attack such creatures. As a result, hezrous are often responsible for frog cults. In remote swamps, frogfolk worship hezrous, who protect their people from mundane and magical dangers in exchange for worship and blood sacrifices. Clever shapechanging druids have used the hezrous’ fondness for frogs to safely communicate with or escape the demons.

monster.name: Hezrou
resistNoteFromTraits: bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The hezrou can breathe air and water.

trait.name: Demonic Resilience
entries: The hezrou is resistant to cold, fire, and lightning damage. In addition, it is immune to poison damage and to the poisoned condition.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The hezrou has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Speak with Frogs and Toads
entries: The hezrou can communicate with frogs and toads as if they shared a language.

trait.name: Standing Leap
entries: The hezrou’s long jump is up to 20 feet and its high jump is up to 10 feet, with or without a running start.

trait.name: Stench
entries: A creature that starts its turn within 10 feet of the hezrou must succeed on a DC 16 CON save or be poisoned until the start of its next turn. On a success, the creature is immune to the hezrou’s Stench for the next 24 hours.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The hezrou makes one Sticky Tongue attack, one Bite attack, and two Claw attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 15 (2d10 + 4) piercing damage.

action.name: Claw
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 17 (3d8 + 4) slashing damage.

action.name: Sticky Tongue
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 20 ft., one target. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) bludgeoning damage, and the target must succeed on a DC 16 STR save or be pulled up to 15 feet toward the hezrou.

bonus.name: Quick Leap
entries: The hezrou jumps horizontally up to 20 feet to an unoccupied space it can see without provoking opportunity attacks. Demon, Husk The formless pile of desiccated hide and broken scales slowly twists and writhes across the ground like a sheet of canvas in the wind. As it draws closer, a guttural syllabic echo drones in a psychic reverberation.

bonus.name: Withered Demons
entries: When left without a purpose or target for their depredations, some lesser demons decay into husk demons rather than devolving into dretches.

bonus.name: Expunged
entries: Little remains of what these demons once were, save for rage and hunger. Husk demons seek to consume the souls of sentient creatures, which can restore the demon’s former shape and glory. They slurp at the bodies and souls of creatures, steadily leeching the vitality and thirst for life from victims.

bonus.name: Tied to the Past
entries: Husk demons inhabit the ruins of demonic strongholds, ancient demonic prisons, or temples on the Material Plane dedicated to dead gods or fiendish exiles. Such sites often contain lost relics and artifacts which the demons owned or tended in life. Experienced scavengers and looters avoid areas inhabited by husk demons, believing the demons to be long-dead guardians pressed into service by the powerful objects themselves. Unfortunate explorers may accidentally wander into such places and find themselves surrounded by these starving fiends.

trait.name: Amorphous
entries: The husk demon can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch wide without squeezing.

trait.name: Demonic Resilience
entries: The husk demon is resistant to cold, fire, and lightning damage. In addition, it is immune to poison damage and to the poisoned condition.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The husk demon has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The husk demon makes two Life Drain attacks.

action.name: Life Drain
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) slashing damage plus 9 (2d8) necrotic damage, and the husk demon regains HP equal to half the necrotic damage dealt. The target must succeed on a DC 15 CON save or its HP maximum is reduced by an amount equal to the necrotic damage taken. This reduction lasts until the creatures finishes a long rest. The target dies if this effect reduces its HP maximum to 0.

bonus.name: Soul Feed
entries: The husk demon feasts on the light in the soul of up to two creatures it can see within 20 feet of it, causing one of the following effects. Each target must succeed on a DC 15 CHA save or suffer the chosen effect. A creature that fails the save against Soul Feed two rounds in a row suffers one level of exhaustion.
• Happiness Feast. The husk demon feasts on the target’s
happiness, causing the target to become crestfallen. The target has disadvantage on attack rolls until the end of its next turn.
• Hope Feast. The husk demon feasts on the target’s hope,
causing the target to become despondent. The target has disadvantage on saves until the end of its next turn.
• Motivation Feast. The husk demon feasts on the target’s

motivation, causing the target to lose its ambitions and become apathetic. The target’s speed is halved until the end of its next turn. Demon, Marilith A green-skinned woman with a serpentine lower body rears up, wielding fiery swords, maces, and daggers in her six arms.

bonus.name: Tactical Demons
entries: Most demon lords have at least one marilith commanding their forces or giving tactical advice. Peerless military strategists, mariliths are sometimes summoned to the Material Plane and bound to lead an army. Many kingdoms have risen to sudden military might after royal wizards bound a marilith to the king's service .

bonus.name: Revel in Combat
entries: Mariliths relish every chance they get to hone their combat skills. When not commanding legions, mariliths enjoy engaging in one-on-one combat or even in combat against several opponents at once.

monster.name: Marilith
resistNoteFromTraits: bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from

trait.name: Demonic Resilience
entries: The marilith is resistant to cold, fire, and lightning damage. In addition, it is immune to poison damage and to the poisoned condition.

trait.name: Flaming Weapons
entries: The marilith’s weapon attacks are magical. When it hits with any weapon, the weapon deals an extra 2d6 fire damage (included in the attack).

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The marilith has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Reactive
entries: The marilith gets up to five extra reactions, but these extra reactions can be used only for Parry.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The marilith makes two Dagger attacks, two Mace attacks, and two Longsword attacks. It can replace two Dagger attacks with a Tail attack.

action.name: Dagger
entries: Melee or Ranged Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft., one target. Hit: 9 (2d4 + 4) piercing damage plus 7 (2d6) fire damage.

action.name: Mace
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) bludgeoning damage plus 7 (2d6) fire damage.

action.name: Longsword
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (2d8 + 4) slashing damage plus 7 (2d6) fire damage.

action.name: Tail
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 10 ft., one creature. Hit: 20 (3d10 + 4) bludgeoning damage plus 7 (2d6) fire damage. If the target is Medium or smaller, it is grappled (escape DC 17). Until this grapple ends, the target is restrained, the marilith can automatically hit the target with its Tail, and the marilith can’t make Tail attacks against other targets.

bonus.name: Marilith Tactics
entries: The marilith shouts one of the following commands at up to two friendly creatures it can see within 30 feet of it:
• March! Each target can use a reaction to move up to
half its speed in a direction of the marilith’s choice. This movement is unaffected by difficult terrain and doesn’t provoke opportunity attacks.
• Protect Yourself! Each target gains the marilith’s Parry
reaction until the start of the marilith’s next turn.
• Remember Your Training! Each target has advantage on
the next weapon attack roll it makes before the start of the marilith’s next turn.

bonus.name: Teleport
entries: The marilith magically teleports, along with any equipment it is wearing or carrying, up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space it can see.

reaction.name: Parry
entries: The marilith adds 5 to its AC against one melee attack that would hit it. To do so, the marilith must see the attacker and be wielding a melee weapon. Demon, Mire Fiend A fiendish, translucent praying mantis with a segmented tail tipped by a stinger rises from the muck, wielding a bamboo spear.

reaction.name: Magical Swamps
entries: Mire fiends were once small, fiendish insects from the swamps of the Abyss that found new homes in magic-blasted swamps in mortal worlds. Over time, the seeping, uncontrolled magic of those swamps helped the creatures grow in size and intelligence, and they now live in social hives.

reaction.name: Intelligent Hunters
entries: Mire fiends are omnivorous, indiscriminate hunters, attacking any prey large enough or plentiful enough to feed the hive. When hunting, mire fiends inject the acid from their stingers into victims, liquefying the innards for easier consumption. A mire fiend might venture out on its own to hunt, but often, the creatures are encountered in small hunting parties.

reaction.name: Coincidental Corruption
entries: Like many demons, mire fiends can corrupt souls, but this corruption happens when they inject acid into the body of a slain mortal. Unable to consume souls, mire fiends don’t capture these corrupted souls, and they often become ghosts, shadows, or specters.

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The mire fiend can breathe air and water.

trait.name: Demonic Resilience
entries: The mire fiend is resistant to cold, fire, and lightning damage. In addition, it is immune to poison damage and to the poisoned condition.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The mire fiend has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The mire fiend has advantage on attack rolls against a creature if at least one of the mire fiend’s allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn’t incapacitated.

trait.name: Swamp Camouflage
entries: The mire fiend’s Stealth is 21 while in swampy terrain.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The mire fiend makes one Mandibles attack, one Spear attack, and one Stinger attack.

action.name: Mandibles
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. Hit: 12 (2d8 + 3) slashing damage.

action.name: Spear
entries: Melee Weapon or Ranged Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) piercing damage, or 6 (1d8 + 2) piercing damage if used with two hands to make a melee attack.

action.name: Stinger
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 10 ft., one creature. Hit: 5 (1d4 + 3) piercing damage plus 10 (3d6) poison damage, and the target must succeed on a DC 14 CON save or be poisoned.

action.name: Stunning Snap (Recharge 5–6)
entries: If the mire fiend has no creature grappled, it snaps its mandibles together with great force, creating a miniature sonic boom in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a DC 14 CON save. On a failure, a creature takes 21 (6d6) thunder damage and is stunned until the end of its next turn. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn’t stunned.

reaction.name: Acidic Retribution
entries: When the mire fiend takes damage, including any damage that reduces it to 0 HP , it can expel acid from its stinger in a 15-foot line that is 5 feet wide. Each creature in that line must make a DC 14 DEX save, taking 10 (3d6) acid damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Demon, Nalfeshnee A fiendish ape with a boar’s head and a vulture’s wings lumbers forward, emitting a strange light.

reaction.name: Gluttonous Demons
entries: Sporting a bizarre mixture of boar and vulture characteristics, nalfeshnee embody gluttony, even stopping in the middle of a fight to feast on a fallen foe. They are always seeking new and unique things to eat. Clever petitioners can secure a temporary alliance with a nalfeshnee by feeding it delicacies it has never tasted, though the alliance ends when the food stops flowing.

reaction.name: Horrific Light
entries: Nalfeshnee emit a strange, scintillating light that disorients and frightens mortal minds. Few mortals can stay close to a nalfeshnee for long, and most suffer after-effects from extended exposure to the light, including nausea, impaired vision, and seeing fleeting images or shadows. Nalfeshnee delight in the discomfort their light creates and find that mortals taste best when devoured while fearful.

monster.name: Nalfeshnee
resistNoteFromTraits: bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from

trait.name: Demonic Resilience
entries: The nalfeshnee is resistant to cold, fire, and lightning damage. In addition, it is immune to poison damage and to the poisoned condition.

trait.name: Horrific Nimbus
entries: The nalfeshnee magically emits scintillating, multicolored light that disorients and frightens mortals. Each creature that isn’t an Aberration, Celestial, or Fiend and that starts its turn within 15 feet of the nalfeshnee must succeed on a DC 16 WIS save or be frightened until the start of its next turn. A creature that fails the save by 5 or more must take the Dash action on its turn and move away from the nalfeshnee by the safest available route, unless there is nowhere to move.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The nalfeshnee has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The nalfeshnee makes one Bite attack, one Gore attack, and two Claw attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 28 (4d8 + 5) piercing damage. If this attack hits a frightened creature, the nalfeshnee regains HP equal to half the damage dealt.

action.name: Claw
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 19 (4d6 + 5) slashing damage.

action.name: Gore
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 21 (3d10 + 5) piercing damage, and the target must succeed on a DC 16 STR save or be knocked prone.

bonus.name: Teleport
entries: The nalfeshnee magically teleports, along with any equipment it is wearing or carrying, up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space it can see.

reaction.name: Sudden Feast
entries: When a creature the nalfeshnee can see within 20 feet of it is reduced to 0 HP or dies, the nalfeshnee can move up to its speed toward the creature without provoking opportunity attacks then feast on the creature, if it moves to within 5 feet of the creature. The nalfeshnee regains HP equal to twice the creature’s CR or class level, and the creature has disadvantage on the next death save it makes, if it is making death saves. Demon, Quasit This miniature fiend has horns jutting from its head, and its oversized bat ears rival the horns in length. It gazes with expressionless, black eyes.

reaction.name: Bullied Demons
entries: Quasits take to mortal worlds to escape bullying from most other demons and to spread Abyssal destruction by serving as familiars. As familiars, quasits goad their masters into vile acts. These acts often start small and simple, slowly escalating until the master has been fully corrupted by the quasit’s influence.

reaction.name: Cowardly Shapechangers
entries: Quasits rarely show their true forms, preferring instead to assume a shape suited for its current environment. The cowardly creatures also rely on their invisibility to escape destruction.

trait.name: Demonic Resilience
entries: The quasit is resistant to cold, fire, and lightning damage. In addition, it is immune to poison damage and to the poisoned condition.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The quasit has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Claws (True Form Only)
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d4 + 3) piercing damage, and the target must succeed on a DC 13 Constitution saving throw or take 5 (2d4) poison damage and be poisoned for 1 minute. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

action.name: Slam (Beast Form Only)
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage (based on the type of damage dealt by the Beast form’s primary attack, such as Bite).

action.name: Invisibility (True Form Only)
entries: The quasit magically turns invisible until it attacks, uses Scare, or uses Change Shape, or until its concentration ends (as if concentrating on a spell). Any equipment the quasit wears or carries is invisible with it.

bonus.name: Change Shape
entries: The quasit magically transforms into a Medium or smaller Beast that has a CR no higher than its own or back into its true form, which is a Fiend. Its statistics, other than its size and speed, are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying transforms with it. It reverts to its true form if it dies.

bonus.name: Scare (1/Day; True Form Only)
entries: One creature of the quasit’s choice within 20 feet of it must succeed on a DC 13 Wisdom saving throw or be frightened for 1 minute. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. QUASIT FAMILIARS Some quasits are willing to serve spellcasters, especially those that enjoy causing mayhem. Such quasits have the following trait.

bonus.name: Familiar
entries: The quasit can serve another creature as a familiar, forming a magic, telepathic bond with that willing companion. While the two are bonded, the companion can sense what the quasit senses as long as they are within 1 mile of each other. While the quasit is within 10 feet of its companion, the companion shares the quasit’s Magic Resistance trait. At any time and for any reason, the quasit can end its service as a familiar, ending the telepathic bond. Demon, Unska Inky runes and tattoos cover the red skin of this demonic creature. Its ink-tipped claws extend to improbable lengths, and horns matted with blood and mud crown its gaunt skull.

bonus.name: Tattooed Heralds
entries: The ink-covered unska demons defile shrines devoted to powerful forces of good by covering them in rude depictions, foul language, and images that venerate the demon lords they serve. While they enjoy befouling places of worship, their strongest drive is to extol the magnificence of their demon lord masters.

bonus.name: Demonic Canvases
entries: Some humanoids etch their skin with blasphemous inscriptions and tattoos, exalting demon lords and fiends. When such a humanoid is executed, the demon lord transforms the dying humanoid into an unska, marking the person as the demon lord’s herald. Demon lords that accept unskas as followers tattoo them with their own mark or sigil, clearly showing the unska’s allegiance.

bonus.name: Magical Tattoos
entries: Unska tattoos are more than simple decoration. An unska can send its tattoos flying off its body and command them to latch onto an opponent. Each unska’s tattoos are different, but most unska can frighten, disorient, or even charm those assaulted by their tattoos. The unska’s ink-stained claws can imbed their ink in creatures, making those creatures more susceptible to the effects of the unska’s magical tattoos.

trait.name: Demonic Resilience
entries: The unska is resistant to cold, fire, and lightning damage. In addition, it is immune to poison damage and to the poisoned condition.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The unska has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The unska makes two Claw attacks and one Tongue attack, or it makes three Ink Blast attacks.

action.name: Claw
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) slashing damage plus 3 (1d6) poison damage, and the target must succeed on a DC 14 CON save or be poisoned for 1 minute. While poisoned, the target has disadvantage on saves against the unska’s Whirl of Ink. The target can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

action.name: Tongue
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 12 (2d8 + 3) piercing damage. The target must succeed on a DC 14 CON save, or its HP maximum is reduced by an amount equal to the damage taken. This reduction lasts until the target finishes a long rest. The target dies if its HP maximum is reduced to 0.

action.name: Ink Blast
entries: Ranged Spell Attack: +5 to hit, range 60 ft., one target. Hit: 12 (3d6 + 2) poison damage, and the target must succeed on a DC 14 CON save or have disadvantage on the next save it makes against the unska’s Whirl of Ink before the start of the unska’s next turn.

bonus.name: Whirl of Ink
entries: Tattoos leap off the unska’s body and latch onto a creature the unska can see within 30 feet of it. The target must succeed on a DC 14 WIS save or be charmed, disoriented, or frightened (the unska’s choice) until the end of its next turn. When a disoriented creature moves, it moves in a random direction. Demon, Vrock Diseased motes flake from the wings of this horrifying mixture of buzzard and scrawny humanoid.

bonus.name: Scavenger Demons
entries: Vrocks are emaciated, bipedal vulture-like fiends that survive off the scraps left behind by mortals and other demons. They are drawn to rotting meat and shiny objects, the latter of which can make a vrock delay an attack or change its flight to acquire. Many mortals have won over or even captured vrocks by offering the demons sparkling trinkets, shining objects, or glowing magical items.

bonus.name: Fungal Spores
entries: Vrocks share a symbiotic relationship with an Abyssal fungus that grows in decaying bodies and in the vrock’s wings. The fungus keeps the vrock’s wings clear of any other debris, allowing it to fly no matter how much filth it acquires while scavenging the wastelands of the Abyss. In return, the vrock provides the fungus with plenty of places to spread its spores.

monster.name: Vrock
resistNoteFromTraits: bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from

trait.name: Demonic Resilience
entries: The vrock is resistant to cold, fire, and lightning damage. In addition, it is immune to poison damage and to the poisoned condition.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The vrock has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The vrock makes one Beak attack and two Talon attacks.

action.name: Beak
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 12 (2d8 + 3) piercing damage.

action.name: Talon
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 14 (2d10 + 3) slashing damage.

action.name: Wing Spores (Recharge 5–6)
entries: The vrock shakes a cloud of toxic spores from its wings. Each creature within 15 feet of it must make a DC 15 CON save. On a failure, a creature takes 28 (8d6) poison damage and is poisoned for 1 minute. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn’t poisoned. While poisoned, a creature takes 3 (1d6) poison damage at the start of each of its turns. A poisoned creature can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. Emptying a vial of holy water on the poisoned creature also ends the effect on it.

bonus.name: Startling Screech
entries: The vrock emits a horrific screech at one creature it can see within 30 feet of it. The target must succeed on a DC 14 CON save or be able to take either an action or bonus action on its turn, not both, and it can’t take reactions. Demon, Wrack Folds of rubber flesh twist between this creature’s unsocketed limbs, and its bones are bent into disturbing angles.

bonus.name: Void-Twisted Fiends
entries: Wrack demons are demons transformed by the energies of the Void where they seep into the Abyss. This most often happens to glabrezu demons, but any lesser demon might be transformed. Disturbingly transformed, their flesh and form lack stability, and the demon’s attack can spread this warping energy into its victims.

bonus.name: Demonic Scouts
entries: Wrack demons serve their demonic lords as scouts and infiltrators. They can rearrange their limbs and flesh as needed to deliver messages, scale walls, and protect themselves. Wrack demons’ connection to the Void also makes interplanar rifts near them more stable. Many demon lords use this to move troops and scouts into other planes, always seeking to expand their empires.

bonus.name: Craven Sycophants
entries: While they are capable combatants, wrack demons hold little power among the demonic legions. If given the opportunity, they avoid battle to focus on objectives they were given by their masters. Unlike other lesser demons, their work grants them access to the ears of powerful demon lords, but the constant attention of a demon lord can be both blessing and curse.

trait.name: Demonic Resilience
entries: The wrack demon is resistant to cold, fire, and lightning damage. In addition, it is immune to poison damage and to the poisoned condition.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The wrack demon has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The wrack demon makes three Slam attacks.

action.name: Slam
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (2d8 + 4) bludgeoning damage, and the target must succeed on a DC 15 CON save or have disadvantage on attack rolls as reality-warping energies of the Void temporarily distort its body.

action.name: Warping Burst (Recharge 5–6)
entries: The wrack demon unleashes a blast of Void energy that warps and distorts the area around the demon. Each creature within 20 feet of the demon must make a DC 15 CON save. On a failure, a creature takes 27 (6d8) necrotic damage, its speed is reduced by 10 feet, and its AC is reduced by 2 until the end of its next turn, as its flesh twists and warps. On a success, a creature takes half the damage, and its speed and AC aren’t reduced.

bonus.name: Body Warping
entries: The wrack demon shifts its malleable form, rearranging limbs, flesh, and bone as needed to fulfill its tasks. It chooses one of the following effects, which lasts until the wrack demon uses this bonus action again:
• Bony Hide. The demon forces the shards of bone to the
surface of its skin, increasing its AC by 2. In addition, it has advantage on checks and saves against any effect that would move it.
• Infiltration. The demon changes the texture and
stickiness of its skin, gaining a climbing speed of 30 feet. In addition, it can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.
• Scout. The demon twists its body into a quadrupedal

form, increasing its speed by 10 feet. In addition, opportunity attacks against it have disadvantage. Derro The short, purple man mumbles absently to himself as he sets a bolt in his crossbow. He lets it loose into the dark cavern ahead and cackles with glee as a screech of pain echoes back to him.

bonus.name: Underworld Dwellers
entries: Deep beneath the earth, the derro gather in clans, thriving in the lightless depths on cavern fungi and on the water from underground lakes. They are short, dwarf-like humanoids with pale hair, blue or purple skin, and white, pupilless eyes. Their unique eyes give them an intense stare, especially when interacting with surface-dwellers, and they use this to their advantage when fighting such creatures. Derro often ally with mycolids and duergar, and they frequently train bats as companions, messengers, and even mounts.

bonus.name: Unique Style
entries: Each derro takes great pride in its hair, braiding, decorating, and dyeing it as a symbol of personal expression. This extends to facial hair with many derro wearing jewelry or even holy symbols in the intricate patterns of their beards. T o many surface dwellers, the wild, spiked hair of a derro warrior lends credence to the stories of derro insanity, but to that warrior, its carefully styled hair might help the derro strike fear into its opponents. These touches can extend beyond the derro’s hair, but even the least expressive of derro takes at least a modicum of care with its hair.

bonus.name: History of Madness
entries: Ancient derro clans worshiped beings that dwell between the stars, and generations of exposure to these beings caused lasting damage to the psyche of the derro, making them resilient against dangers that plague the mind. While many clans devote themselves to deities and demon lords of bats, darkness, and fungi, many others still follow the old ways, worshiping eldritch beings and communing with entities of the Void.

bonus.name: Void Eaters
entries: Some derro take their devotion to eldritch beings and fascination with the Void to a deeper level. These derro, known as void eaters, consume material from the Void or tainted by the Void, believing—somewhat correctly—that this consumption will instill the Void’s power in them. Unfortunately, this also instills a great emptiness in the void eater, leaving it perpetually hungry, and transforms the derro’s face, giving it an over-wide mouth, too many teeth, and a jaw that can unhinge to consume larger meals. A void eater’s connection to the Void keeps it safe from other void-touched creatures, and many less-intelligent void-touched creatures are drawn to the void eater, instinctively following its commands. Many void eaters use this connection to consume more Void energies, eating companions that fall in battle.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The derro has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Sunlight Sensitivity
entries: While in sunlight, the derro has disadvantage on attack rolls, and its Perception is 3 when perceiving by sight.

action.name: Shortsword
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) piercing damage.

action.name: Light Crossbow
entries: Ranged Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, range 80/320 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d8 + 2) piercing damage.

reaction.name: Otherworldly Feast
entries: When a creature the derro can see within 5 feet of it takes damage (including itself), the derro can magically absorb some of the life energy spilling from the creature. The creature that took the damage must succeed on a DC 13 CON save or the derro gains temporary HP equal to half the damage the creature took. BAT RIDERS Many derro ride giant bats to travel quickly between settlements, deliver messages, or attack opponents from the safety of the air. Such derro are often equipped with vials of acid or alchemist’s fire that they drop down on opponents, and they forego wearing leather armor for a specialized riding suit. This suit has small hooks that keep them attached to the furred backs of their mounts, giving them advantage on checks and saves against effects that would knock them prone. The suit also has flaps between the arms and legs, allowing the derro to glide if it falls off its mount. While falling, a derro wearing such a suit can use a reaction to spread its limbs, expanding the suit’s flaps and slowing its rate of descent to 60 feet per round, landing on its feet and taking no falling damage.

bonus.name: Intense Study
entries: The derro intensely studies one creature it can see within 30 feet of it, unnerving the creature. The target must succeed on a DC 12 WIS save or the derro has advantage on the next attack roll it makes against the target before the start of the derro’s next turn.

trait.name: Blood Frenzy
entries: The void eater has advantage on melee attack rolls against any creature that doesn't have all its HP .

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The void eater has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Relentless (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)
entries: If the void eater takes 10 damage or less that would reduce it to 0 HP , it is reduced to 1 HP instead.

trait.name: Sunlight Sensitivity
entries: While in sunlight, the void eater has disadvantage on attack rolls, and its Perception is 3 when perceiving by sight.

trait.name: Void-Touched Weapons
entries: The void eater’s weapon attacks are magical. When the void eater hits with any weapon, the weapon deals an extra 2d6 necrotic damage (included in the attack).

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The devil makes two Claw attacks and one Tail attack, or it makes three Hurl Flame attacks.

action.name: Claw
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) piercing damage.

action.name: Tail
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 12 (2d8 + 3) piercing damage.

action.name: Hurl Flame
entries: Ranged Spell Attack: +5 to hit, range 150 ft., one target. Hit: 12 (3d6 + 2) fire damage. If the target is a flammable object that isn’t being worn or carried, it also catches fire.

bonus.name: Maddening Gaze
entries: The void eater intensely studies one creature it can see within 30 feet of it, purple Void energy flickering across its pale eyes. The target must succeed on a DC 13 WIS save or be frightened of the void eater until the end of its next turn. A creature that fails this save by 5 or more suffers a random, short-term dread effect for 1 minute. Devil, Barbed Spines shoot from nearly every surface of this red fiend, including its tail and spine-tipped hands.

bonus.name: Shock-Troop Devils
entries: Named for the spines that cover its body, the barbed devil serves as a mixture of shock trooper and natural hazard in the hierarchy of devils and devilish armies. When it dies, a spined devil’s body disappears, but it leaves behind its spines, creating a hazard for enemies.

bonus.name: Blood Artistry
entries: Many barbed devils delight in coating their spines in blood, and they eagerly impale creatures they encounter. They use particular patterns of blood on their spines to help distinguish themselves among their fellows. A barbed devil’s favorite “paint” or pattern might change with the day, or the devil might be highly consistent in seeking to create the same pattern weekly. As blood eventually dries and flakes away, barbed devils are always seeking sources of blood to repaint themselves.

bonus.name: Fire Starters
entries: Barbed devils use their infernal fire for arson, finding beauty in fire as well as blood. They are drawn to fiery places and burning buildings, forests, tents, or ships. Devil’s Sight. Magical darkness doesn’t impede the devil’s darkvision.

bonus.name: Devilish Resilience
entries: The devil is resistant to cold damage and to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks. In addition, it is immune to fire damage and poison damage and to the poisoned condition.

bonus.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The devil has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Barbed Death
entries: When the barbed devil dies, its body withers, leaving behind a mass of spikes on the ground in a 10-foot radius centered on the devil’s body. The area becomes difficult terrain for 1 minute, and when a creature moves into or within the area, it takes 2d6 piercing damage for every 5 feet it travels.

trait.name: Barbed Hide
entries: A creature that hits the barbed devil with a melee attack while within 5 feet of it takes 3 (1d6) piercing damage. A creature that starts its turn grappling or grappled by the barbed devil takes 3 (1d6) piercing damage. Devil, Bearded The hulking, bearded fiend swings a glaive with a jagged blade. Its beard starts wriggling, revealing sharp, hairlike spines, as it cackles with sadistic glee.

trait.name: Frontline Devils
entries: Named for the distinctive, prehensile spines that grow on the devil’s chin, bearded devils form the primary fighting force of devilish armies. More powerful than imps and more skilled than lemures, they form one of the lowest rungs in the devilish hierarchy and are bound to serve greater devils. They vie among themselves for a chance to gain the notice of a pit fiend or archdevil. As a result, they throw themselves wholly into any fight or task, knowing there is glory in death, status in victory, and a lifetime as a lemure in failure.

trait.name: Delight in Pain
entries: Bearded devils greatly enjoy inflicting pain on others and relish every chance they get to do so. Their glaives inflict wounds that worsen with each cut, and their spined beards prevent wounds from healing, making these lowly devils formidable foes.

trait.name: Text
entries: Devil’s Sight. Magical darkness doesn’t impede the devil’s darkvision.

trait.name: Devilish Resilience
entries: The devil is resistant to cold damage and to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks. In addition, it is immune to fire damage and poison damage and to the poisoned condition.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The devil has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Show Mettle
entries: While it can see a superior devil, such as a barbed devil, pit fiend, or similar, the bearded devil has advantage on saves against being charmed and frightened.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The devil makes two Beard Spines attacks and one Glaive attack.

action.name: Beard Spines
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. Hit: 8 (2d4 + 3) piercing damage, and the target must succeed on a DC 13 CON save or be poisoned for 1 minute. While poisoned in this way, the target can’t regain HP . The target can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

action.name: Glaive
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (1d10 + 3) slashing damage. If the target is a creature other than a Construct or Undead, it must succeed on a DC 13 CON save or lose 3 (1d6) HP at the start of each of its turns due to an infernal wound. Each time the devil hits the wounded target with this attack, the damage dealt by the wound increases by 3 (1d6). Any creature can take an action to stanch the wound with a successful DC 10 WIS (Medicine) check. The wound also closes if the target receives magical healing, provided the target is capable of regaining HP .

bonus.name: Enhance Pain
entries: The bearded devil enhances the pain in one creature it can see within 30 feet of it that is poisoned from the devil’s Beard Spines attack or suffering an infernal wound from the devil’s Glaive attack. The target must succeed on a DC 13 CON save or be incapacitated until the end of its next turn, as it doubles over in pain. Devil, Bone The chitter of large insect wings echoes around a gaunt, skeletal fiend as it floats over a hellscape. Its scorpion tail lashes side to side, dripping with poison.

bonus.name: Enforcer Devils
entries: Named for their skeletal appearance, these devils are tasked with enforcing devilish laws and ensuring devils don’t step out of line. They take pleasure in reporting transgressions to their superiors, especially if it raises their standing at the cost of a peer’s. Bone devils aren’t unreasonable, however. While they must report the deeds of the devils they encounter, many bone devils play favorites. When lesser devils are particularly obedient or loyal to them, bone devils ensure these devils rise in the ranks with them, grooming their own loyal cadre as the devil grows in power. Many prominent pit fiends and even some archdevils are said to have once been bone devils who rose in power by surrounding themselves with loyal followers.

bonus.name: Cruel Taskmasters
entries: The bone devils’ increased sense of importance often makes them particularly nasty and cruel in their treatment of lesser devils. They delight in commanding lesser devils to perform various tasks across the Hells, some of their own cruel devising and others passed down from superior devils. As the lesser devils rarely know the source of the command, most adhere to a bone devil’s demands—regardless of how difficult, inane, or dangerous—hoping to raise their own station.

trait.name: Text
entries: Devil’s Sight. Magical darkness doesn’t impede the devil’s darkvision.

trait.name: Devilish Resilience
entries: The devil is resistant to cold damage and to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks. In addition, it is immune to fire damage and poison damage and to the poisoned condition.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The devil has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The devil makes two Claw attacks and one Sting attack.

action.name: Claw
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 17 (3d8 + 4) slashing damage.

action.name: Sting
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 15 (2d10 + 4) piercing damage plus 14 (4d6) poison damage, and the target must succeed on a DC 15 CON save or be poisoned for 1 minute. The target can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

bonus.name: Devilish Command
entries: The bone devil shouts one of the following commands at one creature it can see within 30 feet of it. The target must succeed on a DC 15 CHA save or obey the command. A lesser devil, such as a barbed devil or lemure, has disadvantage on this save.
• Attack. The target uses its reaction to make one melee
or ranged attack (the devil’s choice) against one creature the target can see within its weapon’s range.
• Beg for Mercy. The target immediately falls prone and
can’t cast spells with verbal components on its next turn as it is magically compelled to speak pleas of mercy.
• Flee. The target uses its reaction to move up to its speed

directly away from the bone devil. This movement doesn’t provoke opportunity attacks, but the target doesn’t avoid damaging terrain, such as lava. Devil, Chain A porcelain mask with a constantly shifting face sits atop a curtain of barbed chains in a vaguely humanoid shape. With each step, its form shifts, chains rattle, and screams of terror echo.

bonus.name: Torturer Devils
entries: Named for the chains that cover their slim bodies, these devils exist to torture mortal souls, lost travelers, and disobedient devils. As the punishers of the Hells, they sit outside of the hierarchy, punishing greater and lesser devils alike. Only archdevils, who are their direct superiors, escape the art of these devils.

bonus.name: Terrifying Mask
entries: A chain devil’s face is covered in a mask made of the devil’s own flesh and bones, appearing porcelain. Though the mask appears to be floating above the chains surrounding the devil, it is in fact part of the devil’s own face and can’t be removed. As torturers, chain devils instinctively sense what would cause the most terror in a victim and can reshape their masks to resemble that frightening thing, whether it is the face of a hated enemy, dying loved one, or nightmarish horror.

trait.name: Text
entries: Devil’s Sight. Magical darkness doesn’t impede the devil’s darkvision.

trait.name: Devilish Resilience
entries: The devil is resistant to cold damage and to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks. In addition, it is immune to fire damage and poison damage and to the poisoned condition.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The devil has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The devil makes four Chain attacks.

action.name: Chain
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (1d8 + 4) slashing damage plus 7 (2d6) piercing damage, and the target is grappled (escape DC 15). Until the grapple ends, the target is restrained and takes 14 (4d6) piercing damage at the start of each of its turns, and the chain devil can’t use the same Chain on another target.

bonus.name: Animate Chains
entries: The chain devil commands chains it can see within 60 feet of it that aren’t being worn or carried to grab up to two creatures it can see within 10 feet of at least one commanded chain. Each creature must succeed on a DC 15 DEX save or be restrained. A creature, including the restrained creature, can take its action to free the restrained creature by succeeding on a DC 15 STR check. The devil can have up to two creatures restrained in this way at a time. If it uses this bonus action to restrain a third, the effect ends on one of the previous targets (the devil’s choice).

reaction.name: Unnerving Mask
entries: When a creature the devil can see starts its turn within 30 feet of the devil, the devil can command its mask to shift to look like one of the creature’s departed loved ones or bitter enemies. If the creature can see the mask, it must succeed on a DC 15 WIS save or be frightened until the end of its turn. Devil, Erinyes This beautiful, winged fiend’s cold, calculating eyes and curved horns are at odds with its angelic appearance.

reaction.name: Warrior Devils
entries: Some of the greatest warriors in the Hells, erinyes dive into battle with blazing swords and gleaming armor. Disciplined and skilled fighters, erinyes rarely back down from a fight unless overwhelmed, and they adjust their strategies as needed to counter the strengths of their opponents. When archdevils have minor disputes that don’t require sending entire armies, they often send erinyes as champions to resolve the disputes.

reaction.name: Fallen Angels
entries: The first erinyes were angels that succumbed to the lure of an archdevil of battle, who promised them more worthy fights than those provided under their angelic commander. Erinyes have since become devils in their own right, carving out their own place within devilish society. Their past as angels still follows erinyes, and many relish the chance to test their might against angels they encounter, often fighting to the death when facing such creatures.

trait.name: Devilish Resilience
entries: The devil is resistant to cold damage and to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks. In addition, it is immune to fire damage and poison damage and to the poisoned condition.

trait.name: Disciplined Fighter
entries: At the start of each of its turns, the erinyes chooses one of the following fighting styles to adopt, which lasts until the start of the erinyes’s next turn.
• Aggressive. The erinyes has advantage on the first attack
it makes on its turn, but the next attack roll against the erinyes has advantage.
• Retributive. The next time the erinyes is hit by a melee
weapon attack from a creature within 5 feet of it, the erinyes can make one Longsword attack against the attacker (no action required).
• Defensive. The next time erinyes uses the Parry reaction,
it takes only half the damage if the attack hits and regains 18 (4d8) HP if the attack doesn’t hit.

trait.name: Poisonous Weapons
entries: The erinyes’s weapon attacks are magical. When the erinyes hits with any weapon, the weapon deals an extra 4d8 poison damage (included in the attack).

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The erinyes has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The erinyes makes three Longsword or Longbow attacks.

action.name: Longsword
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (1d8 + 4) slashing damage, or 9 (1d10 + 4) slashing damage if used with two hands, plus 18 (4d8) poison damage.

action.name: Longbow
entries: Ranged Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, range 150/600 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) piercing damage plus 18 (4d8) poison damage, and the target must succeed on a DC 16 CON save or be poisoned for 1 minute. The target can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

reaction.name: Parry
entries: The erinyes adds 4 to its AC against one melee attack that would hit it. To do so, the erinyes must see the attacker and be wielding a melee weapon. Devil, Horned A pair of curved ram’s horns sweep out from this winged, red-skinned fiend’s brow. Its sinuous, spear-tipped tail glows with unholy energies.

reaction.name: Lieutenant Devils
entries: Named for the massive horns that crown their heads, these lieutenants of devilish armies can be found on the front lines, fighting alongside their troops or stabbing their deadly tails into the fray from above. They care more for the thrill of battle than the direction of troops, but horned devils don’t tolerate deserters or troops unwilling to pull their own weight.

reaction.name: Brutal Combatants
entries: Horned devils thrive amidst bloodshed, slamming their horned heads into opponents and causing terrible wounds with their tails. The wounds inflicted by the devil’s tail don’t heal properly and burn with a hellish infection, worsening with each stab.

trait.name: Text
entries: Devil’s Sight. Magical darkness doesn’t impede the devil’s darkvision.

trait.name: Devilish Resilience
entries: The devil is resistant to cold damage and to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks. In addition, it is immune to fire damage and poison damage and to the poisoned condition.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The devil has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The devil makes two Fork attacks and one Tail attack, or it makes three Hurl Flame attacks.

action.name: Fork
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 19 (3d8 + 6) piercing damage.

action.name: Tail
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 22 (3d10 + 6) piercing damage. If the target is a creature other than a Construct or Undead, it must succeed on a DC 17 CON save or lose 10 (3d6) HP at the start of each of its turns due to an infernal wound. Each time the devil hits the wounded target with this attack, the damage dealt by the wound increases by 10 (3d6). Any creature can take an action to stanch the wound with a successful DC 12 WIS (Medicine) check. The wound also closes if the target receives magical healing.

action.name: Hurl Flame
entries: Ranged Spell Attack: +7 to hit, range 150 ft., one target. Hit: 20 (5d6 + 3) fire damage. If the target is a flammable object that isn’t being worn or carried, it also catches fire.

bonus.name: Headbutt
entries: The horned devil slams its horned head into a creature it can see within 5 feet of it. The target must make a DC 17 STR save. On a failure, the target takes 7 (2d6) bludgeoning damage and is knocked prone. On a success, the target takes half the damage and isn’t knocked prone. Devil, Ice A flat, bulbous head shades the creature’s eyes from the glare of the sunlit snow as it surveys the frozen landscape.
