The Construct remains for 1 hour, until the tinkerer dies, or until the tinkerer dismisses it as a bonus action. When the Construct dies or is dismissed, it falls into its component parts.

bonus.name: Scurry
entries: The kobold moves up to 15 feet without provoking opportunity attacks. If the kobold is aware of traps in the area, the kobold can choose if this movement triggers any of them.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The kobold has advantage on attack rolls against a creature if at least one of the kobold’s allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn’t incapacitated.

trait.name: Sunlight Sensitivity
entries: While in sunlight, the kobold has disadvantage on attack rolls, and its Perception is 7 when perceiving by sight.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The kobold witch makes three Eldritch Burst attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Spellcasting.

action.name: Eldritch Burst
entries: Melee or Ranged Spell Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 60 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (2d8 + 4) force damage.

action.name: Spellcasting
entries: The kobold witch casts one of the following spells, using CHA as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 15).
At will: disguise self, druidcraft, guidance
2/day each: command, ray of enfeeblement
1/day: bestow curse

bonus.name: Scurry
entries: The kobold moves up to 15 feet without provoking opportunity attacks. If the kobold is aware of traps in the area, the kobold can choose if this movement triggers any of them. Witch’s Cauldron. The kobold witch ladles steaming brew out of a small cauldron it is holding and flings the substance at one friendly creature it can see within 15 feet of it. The target gains 5 (2d4) temporary HP until the start of the witch’s next turn.

reaction.name: Hex
entries: When a creature the kobold witch can see within 60 feet of it makes an ability check, attack roll, or save, the target must roll a d6 and subtract the number rolled from that ability check, attack roll, or save. Kraken The massive, squid-like creature breaks the surface of the water, each tentacle as long as a galleon.

reaction.name: Primordial Terror
entries: Krakens are massive, deep sea-dwelling leviathans possessing a great intellect. Many are obsessed with destroying any land- or air-dwelling creatures that ply the ocean’s waves, and untold sailors, fisherfolk, and seaside villagers have perished in their writhing tentacles. Thankfully, krakens are rarely found in pairs or groups .

reaction.name: God Hunter, Ship Killer
entries: A single kraken is strong enough to destroy the largest ships and even threaten a seaside community. Krakens were once servants of oceanic deities, but some ancient schism led to their separation from the divine. Most reject their divine parent and attack any priests, paladins, or celestials they encounter.

reaction.name: Undersea Hoard
entries: Krakens sleep in deep-sea caverns, often for decades at a time. At the heart of a kraken’s lair lies the plunder from eons of crushed ships, sunken cities, and lost civilizations. The ships, marble pillars, and other elements of its hoard are often arranged like a diorama in a broken and rotten mockery of what they once were. A KRAKEN’S LAIR Krakens keep lairs in deep ocean trenches and remote, submerged coastal caverns, where they can slumber for years at a time undisturbed. A kraken’s lair typically includes dozens of tunnels, where the kraken can easily hide or corner any intruders, with a central treasure chamber. LAIR ACTIONS On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the kraken can take a lair action to cause one of the following effects; the kraken can’t use the same effect two rounds in a row:
• Fulminate. The kraken’s lair becomes electrified. Up

to four creatures the kraken can see within 120 feet of it must make a DC 22 CON save, taking 13 (3d8) lightning damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. A creature that fails the save by 5 or more is also stunned until the end of its next turn.
• Silt Cloud. A 60-foot-radius sphere of fine, swirling

sand appears centered on a point the kraken can see within 120 feet of it. The sphere spreads around corners and is heavily obscured. The cloud remains for 10 minutes or until the kraken uses this lair action again.
• Tidal Churn. Water shifts in the kraken’s lair. Up to

four creatures the kraken can see within 60 feet of it that are touching water must succeed on a DC 22 STR save or be pushed or pulled up to 60 feet away from or toward the kraken (the kraken’s choice). REGIONAL EFFECTS The region containing a kraken’s lair is altered by its presence, which creates one or more of the following effects:
• Dark Brine. Water within 5 miles of the kraken’s lair
becomes supernaturally dark, silty, or otherwise lightly obscured.
• Thunderous Waves. Within 10 miles of the kraken’s

lair, waves and seaborn winds produce a deafening roar. Each hour a creature spends in the area, it must succeed on a DC 13 CON save or be deafened. Creatures submerged in water or sand automatically succeed on the save. In addition, each creature that spends at least half of a short or long rest in the area and not submerged in water or sand must succeed on a DC 13 CON save or gain no benefit from the rest.
• Tide Sense. The kraken is aware of the presence of any

creature within 1 mile of the lair that remains in contact with water or sand for at least 1 hour. In addition, the kraken knows the distance and direction of each Celestial and Fiend in the area. If the kraken dies, these effects fade in 1d10 days.

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The kraken can breathe air and water.

trait.name: Freedom of Movement
entries: The kraken ignores difficult terrain, and magical effects can’t reduce its speed or cause it to be restrained. It can spend 5 feet of movement to escape from nonmagical restraints or being grappled.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the kraken fails a save, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Monstrosity Resilience
entries: The kraken is resistant to exhaustion and to the frightened condition.

trait.name: Siege Monster
entries: The kraken deals double damage to objects and structures.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The kraken makes three Tentacle attacks. It can replace one Tentacle attack with a Bite attack.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +17 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 28 (4d8 + 10) piercing damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature grappled by the kraken, that creature is swallowed, and the grapple ends. While swallowed, the creature is blinded and restrained, it has total cover against attacks and other effects outside the kraken, and it takes 42 (12d6) acid damage at the start of each of the kraken’s turns. If the kraken takes 50 damage or more on a single turn from a creature inside it, the kraken must succeed on a DC 24 CON save at the end of that turn or regurgitate all swallowed creatures, which fall prone in a space within 10 feet of the kraken. If the kraken dies, a swallowed creature is no longer restrained by it and can escape from the corpse using 15 feet of movement, exiting prone.

action.name: Tentacle
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +17 to hit, reach 30 ft., one target. Hit: 24 (4d6 + 10) bludgeoning damage, and the target is grappled (escape DC 22). Until this grapple ends, the target is restrained. The kraken has ten tentacles, each of which can grapple one target.

action.name: Ink Cloud (Recharge 6)
entries: While underwater, the kraken expels an ink cloud in a 30-foot-radius centered on the kraken. The cloud spreads around corners, and the area is heavily obscured. Each creature in the area when it first appears must make a DC 22 CON save, taking 110 (20d10) poison damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. A creature that enters the ink cloud for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there must succeed on a DC 22 CON save or take 11 (2d10) poison damage and be poisoned until the start of its next turn. The cloud lasts for 1 minute or until dispersed by a strong current.

bonus.name: Fling
entries: One Large or smaller object held or creature grappled by the kraken is thrown up to 60 feet in a random direction and knocked prone. If a thrown target strikes a solid surface, the target takes 3 (1d6) bludgeoning damage for every 10 feet it was thrown. If the target is thrown at another creature, that creature must succeed on a DC 22 DEX save or take the same damage and be knocked prone.

bonus.name: Reel
entries: The kraken pulls up to two creatures grappled by it up to 25 feet straight toward it.

legendary.name: Text
entries: The kraken can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action option can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature’s turn. The kraken regains spent legendary actions at the start of its turn.

legendary.name: Swim
entries: The kraken swims up to half its swimming speed without provoking opportunity attacks.

legendary.name: Tentacle Attack
entries: The kraken makes one Tentacle attack.

legendary.name: Lightning Storm (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The kraken magically calls bolts of lightning to strike up to two creatures it can see or sense within 120 feet of it. Each creature must make a DC 22 DEX save, taking 22 (4d10) lightning damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Lamia The sounds of tinkling bells through the arid hills precedes the half-goat, half-human creature.

legendary.name: Prisoners of Immortality
entries: Most lamia descend from a group of corrupt mages who bargained for immortality with the powers of darkness. Betrayed by the fiends, the lamia gained their wish but became half-beast. The first lamia tried to return to human society, but their immortality made it difficult to keep friends and allies and to avoid anger and suspicion. Because of this, most lamia inhabit badlands, barren foothills, and other arid locations, but some travel the world, carrying their wealth in jewelry, fine clothes, and small treasures secreted on their person.

legendary.name: Badlands Danger
entries: Lamia are swift and dangerous creatures typically found in ancient ruins and the crumbling remains of older civilizations. Their innate magic makes them notoriously difficult to track, as their tracks seem to erase after each step, and they can confound a victim’s mind with both arcane illusions and compulsions.

legendary.name: Eager for Refinement
entries: Lamia are hedonists and desire finery in all its forms. Their small, cramped hill lairs in caverns or castle ruins are magnificently appointed and decorated with goods taken from caravans and travelers. For a lamia to agree to hold an audience with visitors requires that a petitioner display good manners and bring a gift that sparkles or dazzles in some way. These gifts need not be more than a token: even threadbare elegance is often enough, though older lamia can certainly tell copper from gold and threadbare from new.

trait.name: Badlands Strider
entries: Difficult terrain composed of sand, mud, or rock doesn’t cost the lamia extra movement. In addition, the lamia leaves no tracks or other traces of its passage when moving through sandy, muddy, or rocky terrain.

trait.name: Monstrosity Resilience
entries: The lamia is resistant to exhaustion and to the frightened condition.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The lamia makes two Hooves attacks and one Dagger attack, or it makes three Arcane Bolt attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Spellcasting.

action.name: Dagger
entries: Melee or Ranged Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d4 + 3) piercing damage.

action.name: Hooves
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 14 (2d10 + 3) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Arcane Bolt
entries: Ranged Spell Attack: +5 to hit, range 60 ft., one target. Hit: 12 (2d8 + 3) force damage.

action.name: Spellcasting
entries: The lamia casts one of the following spells, requiring no material components and using CHA as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 13).
At will: minor illusion, silent image, suggestion
3/day each: charm, command, major image
1/day: compulsion

bonus.name: Intoxicating Gaze
entries: The lamia gazes at one creature it can see within 30 feet of it. The target must succeed on a DC 13 CHA save or be charmed until the end of its next turn and cursed for 1 hour. While cursed, the target has disadvantage on WIS saves and on all ability checks. Lantern Hagfish A writhing slug-shaped clump of wrinkled flesh weaves through clusters of slime-crusted bone. As its form contorts, its pores leak viscous slime that glows with pale, otherworldly light.

bonus.name: Void Worms
entries: Lantern hagfish are worm-like creatures native to worlds stricken by the Void—indeed, they are one of the few creatures capable of surviving such darkened places. They produce a luminescent slime that protects their bodies from the hazards of the Void and allows them to survive indefinitely in a dormant state when food is scarce. They can be found on the Material Plane in rivers, lakes, and seas where the barrier between the mortal world and the Void is thin. Lantern hagfish in the mortal world often gorge themselves on fish, crustaceans, and even coastal animals, fattening themselves on the bounty of the plane before finding their way back to the Void.

bonus.name: Glowing Communication
entries: Lantern hagfish can change the color and brightness of their slime’s luminescence, and they use this to communicate with each other. They most often communicate tides or currents or share when plentiful food is nearby. When gathered, they can use their lights to coordinate takedowns of larger prey. Creatures that frequently travel the Void worlds, such as satarre, use captured lantern hagfish to provide light or warn them of void waves or tremors.

trait.name: Aberrant Resilience
entries: The lantern hagfish is resistant to the charmed, frightened, paralyzed, and stunned conditions, and it has advantage on saves against spells or effects that would alter its form.

trait.name: Bioluminescent Slime
entries: The lantern hagfish sheds bright light in a 5- to 20-foot radius and dim light for an additional number of feet equal to the chosen radius. The hagfish can alter the radius as a bonus action. In addition, the hagfish has advantage on attack rolls against a creature if at least one other hagfish is within 5 feet of the creature and that other hagfish isn’t incapacitated.

trait.name: Void Dweller
entries: The lantern hagfish doesn’t require air or sleep, and it is immune to the effects of Void terrain. In addition, it can swim through the Void as if through water.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +3 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 3 (1d4 + 1) piercing damage. If the target is a prone creature that isn’t a Construct or Undead, the hagfish can burrow into the creature’s flesh. While burrowed into a creature, the hagfish can’t attack, and at the start of each of the hagfish’s turns, the target loses 3 (1d4 + 1) HP as the hagfish consumes it from the inside. The burrowed hagfish moves with the target whenever the target moves, requiring none of the hagfish’s movement. It can detach itself by spending 5 feet of its movement on its turn. It does so after it consumes 6 HP worth of flesh from the target or the target dies. A creature, including the target, can take its action to extract the burrowed hagfish by succeeding on a DC 11 WIS (Medicine) check.

bonus.name: Slime Slide
entries: While on a solid surface, the lantern hagfish slides up to 15 feet in a straight line and can move through the space of any creature without provoking opportunity attacks. Each space on that surface along that line is coated in slime until the start of the hagfish’s next turn. Each creature in that line, and any creature that enters a slime-coated space for the first time on a turn must succeed on a DC 11 DEX save or fall prone. Lich Clad in the finest raiment, this skeletal spellcaster floats on currents of immense power. The light of its arcane might shines from the hollow eye sockets of its bare skull.

bonus.name: Eternal Scholar
entries: A lich is a spellcaster who seeks immortality out of fear of death, to continue lifelong research, and as a path to greater power. However, even the most megalomaniac and least scholarly liches spend years researching how to become a lich, which varies for each lich.

bonus.name: Soul Vessels
entries: Regardless of the lich’s road to lichdom, the final step always requires complex and blasphemous rituals that bind the lich’s soul into a vessel called a phylactery. The lich’s mortal body then becomes undead, and its soul stays safe within the phylactery. A lich can only truly be killed if its phylactery is destroyed, leading most liches to go to great lengths to safeguard their phylacteries.

bonus.name: Centuries-Long Plots
entries: A lich’s immortality helps it execute far-reaching and long-lasting plans, manipulating entire cities and nations. In the process, the lich also continues its arcane studies, amassing a collection of spells, magic items, and relics to enhance its power. The longer lived a lich is, the harder it is for most mortal creatures to comprehend the scope of its plans.

bonus.name: Powerful Necromancers
entries: Even liches not well- versed in necromancy learn a great deal about it by becoming a lich. As powerful undead, many lesser undead are drawn to liches, leaving even the most isolated liches with several undead minions at its disposal. Similarly, spellcasters seeking arcane knowledge often opt to serve liches to learn from them. Wise apprentices leave before becoming powerful enough to be considered a threat. A LICH’S LAIR Preferring to pursue their goals in solitude, most liches create lairs in isolated towers, long-abandoned mansions, or hidden dungeons. Their lairs are filled with magical and mundane traps, undead minions, arcane wards, and magical sensors that allow the lich to observe any area of its lair. LAIR ACTIONS On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the lich can take a lair action to cause one of the following effects; the lich can’t use the same effect two rounds in a row:
• Abjure Life. Each creature within 120 feet of the lich
that isn’t undead can’t regain HP until initiative count 20 on the next round. For the duration, a creature that is reduced to 0 HP within 120 feet of the lich must immediately make a death save.
• Shroud of the Dead. A blanket of necrotic energy falls

on a point the lich can see within 120 feet of it. Each creature within 10 feet of that point must succeed on a DC 20 WIS save or be blinded and deafened and vulnerable to necrotic damage until initiative count 20 on the next round.
• Unlife Tether. One creature the lich can see within

60 feet of it must succeed on a DC 20 CHA save or become magically tethered to the lich until initiative count 20 on the next round. While tethered, the lich is resistant to all damage dealt by the tethered creature, the tethered creature takes necrotic damage equal to half the damage taken by the lich, and if the tethered creature regains HP , the lich regains twice that amount. REGIONAL EFFECTS The region containing a lich’s lair is warped by its influence, which creates one or more of the following effects:
• Scrambled Translocation. Within 1 mile of the lich’s

lair, planar travel and teleportation are warped. When a creature other than the lich tries to teleport into, out of, or within the area, the creature must roll a d6. On a 1–3, the teleportation fails. On a roll of 4–6, a teleporting creature takes 11 (2d10) force damage and appears in an unoccupied space just outside of this area.
• Spell Detection. The lich is intrinsically aware of and
can identify any spell cast within 3 miles of its lair. In addition, if a creature casts a spell of 4th circle or higher, the lich knows the distance and direction of that creature for 1 hour.
• Withering Fog. A magical fog permeates the area

within 5 miles of the lich’s lair. The area is lightly obscured, and each creature that isn’t a Construct or Undead that finishes a long rest in the area must succeed on a DC 13 CON save or suffer one level of exhaustion. If the lich dies, these effects fade in 1d10 days.

monster.name: Lich
languages: Common plus up to five other languages
immuneNoteFromTraits: bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from

trait.name: Arcane Mastery
entries: The lich can concentrate on two spells simultaneously. If it casts a third spell that requires concentration, the lich loses concentration on the oldest spell. If the lich is concentrating on two spells and loses concentration because of taking damage, it loses concentration only on the oldest spell.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the lich fails a save, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Rejuvenation
entries: If it has a phylactery, a destroyed lich gains a new body in 1d10 days, regaining all its HP and becoming active again. The new body appears within 5 feet of the phylactery.

trait.name: Turn Resistance
entries: The lich has advantage on saves against any effect that turns undead.

trait.name: Undead Nature
entries: The lich doesn’t require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Undead Resilience
entries: The lich is immune to poison damage, to exhaustion, and to the poisoned condition.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The lich uses Lich’s Gaze. It then makes four Arcane Death Burst attacks, or it makes three Arcane Death Burst attacks and one Death-Infused Rod attack. It can replace one attack with a use of Spellcasting.

action.name: Arcane Death Burst
entries: Melee or Ranged Spell Attack: +12 to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 120 ft., one target. Hit: 23 (4d8 + 5) force damage plus 17 (5d6) necrotic damage.

action.name: Death-Infused Rod
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 21 (4d8 + 3) bludgeoning damage plus 17 (5d6) necrotic damage, and the target must succeed on a DC 20 CON save or be paralyzed for 1 minute. The target can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. Lich’s Gaze. The lich fixes its gaze on one creature it can see within 30 feet of it. The target must succeed on a DC 20 CHA save or be charmed or frightened (the lich’s choice) until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Spellcasting
entries: The lich casts one of the following spells, using INT as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 20).
At will: detect magic, disguise self, mage hand, message,
prestidigitation
3/day each: charm, detect thoughts, dispel magic,
invisibility (self only), mirror image
2/day each: bestow curse, cloudkill, dimension door,
greater hold
1/day each: plane shift (self only), power word stun,
wall of force

bonus.name: Levitate
entries: The lich can rise or descend vertically up to 20 feet and can remain suspended there. This bonus action works like the levitate spell, except there is no duration, and the lich doesn’t need to concentrate to continue levitating each round.

reaction.name: Absorb Spell
entries: When a creature the lich can see within 60 feet of it casts a spell, the lich can absorb the spell’s energy, countering it. This works like the counterspell spell, except the lich must always make a spellcasting ability check, no matter the spell's circle. Its ability check for this is +12. If it successfully counters the spell, the lich gains 5 temporary HP for each level of the spell.

legendary.name: Text
entries: The lich can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action option can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature’s turn. The lich regains spent legendary actions at the start of its turn.

legendary.name: Move
entries: The lich moves up to its speed without provoking opportunity attacks. If the lich is levitating, it can use this legendary action to move horizontally, propelling itself with a blast of arcane power.

legendary.name: Cast a Spell (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The lich uses Spellcasting.

legendary.name: Disrupt Life (Costs 3 Actions)
entries: Each creature within 20 feet of the lich that isn’t Undead must make a DC 20 CON save, taking 21 (6d6) necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. The lich regains HP equal to the single highest amount of necrotic damage dealt. Lich, Virtuoso The beautiful singer bows to the adoring crowd before stepping behind the curtain. Away from the eyes of the crowd, the singer changes its half-mask, briefly revealing a ghastly, undead visage.

legendary.name: Birthed by Art
entries: A virtuoso lich is an artist whose love of art sustains it beyond death. Such a lich is created when an artist powerful in both its artistic and magical expression dies with art left undone. The artists often die before completing or creating a masterpiece, and the torment of the art left undone couples with the artist’s powerful magical talents, turning the artist into a virtuoso lich. A virtuoso lich’s soul is bound to an object of art, such as a favorite musical instrument, painting, dance slippers, quill, or some other object of artistic expression that was significant to the lich in life. This piece of art is the lich’s phylactery.

legendary.name: Beautiful Mien
entries: A virtuoso lich maintains the beauty of its former life, appearing much as it did in life—save for one physical feature that betrays its undead nature. This undead feature can be a clawed, skeletal hand, which the lich hides in a glove; a stiff, zombie-like leg, which the lich disguises with robes and a cane; a face ravaged by undeath, which the lich covers in a beautiful mask; or any other appropriate feature. A VIRTUOSO LICH’S LAIR A virtuoso lich chooses a lair with an eye and ear for artistic potential, whether that lair is an ancient cavern with natural acoustics, a meadow with plentiful natural light, a hall of mirrors, or some other locale capable of enhancing some form of art, allowing the lich’s magic and artistic expression to swell, reaching every corner. LAIR ACTIONS On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the virtuoso lich takes a lair action to cause one of the following effects; the lich can’t use the same effect two rounds in a row:
• Artistic Assault. The virtuoso lich channels an artistic

expression it can see or hear into a magical assault. The artistic expression must be of the type chosen with the lich’s Versatile Artist trait, but it otherwise can be any form of expression not originating from the lich, such as the song of nearby singers that echoes in the lair, the colorful paint decorating canvases, the twirling forms of dancers, or similar. The lich chooses a creature it can see within 30 feet of the artistic expression. The target must make a DC 17 DEX save, taking 18 (4d8) damage of the type chosen with the Versatile Artist trait on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.
• Artistic Distraction. The natural artistry of the

lich’s lair becomes suddenly enhanced, distracting and hindering nearby creatures. The lich chooses a point it can see within 60 feet of it. Each creature within 5 feet of that point must make a DC 17 CHA save. On a failure, a creature has disadvantage on saves against the lich’s spells and its Corrupted Art action until initiative count 20 on the next round.
• Veil of Art. A shimmering veil of an artistic expression

of the lich’s choice, such as dancing, music, swirling colors, or similar, appears on a point the lich can see within 60 feet of it. Each creature within 10 feet of that point must succeed on a DC 17 WIS save or be incapacitated until initiative count 20 on the next round, as it spends most of its turn mesmerized by the artistic expression. REGIONAL EFFECTS The region containing a virtuoso lich’s lair is warped by its magical influence, which creates one or more of the following effects:
• Artistic Charm. Each nonhostile Beast within 1 mile

of the lich’s lair that is exposed to an artist performing a form of art, such as dancing, playing music, or painting, must succeed on a DC 13 CHA save or become friendly toward the artist. The Beast doesn’t obey the artist’s commands, but it doesn’t attack the artist unless attacked by the artist or the artist’s allies.
• Inspire Art. Creatures within 5 miles of the lich’s lair
have advantage on CHA (Performance) checks when performing the art the lich has chosen that day as part of its Versatile Artist trait.
• Vibrant Art. Artistic expressions within 10 miles of the

lair are more vibrant: music has more body, paint colors are more vibrant, tailored clothing fits perfectly, and similar. Because of this vibrancy, Humanoids in the area and exposed to such art are more likely to be pleasant and in good moods and less likely to act aggressively. If the lich dies, these effects fade in 1d10 days.

monster.name: Virtuoso Lich
languages: Common plus up to two other languages

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the lich fails a save, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Rejuvenation
entries: If it has a phylactery, a destroyed lich gains a new body in 1d10 days, regaining all its HP and becoming active again. The new body appears within 5 feet of the phylactery.

trait.name: Turn Resistance
entries: The lich has advantage on saves against any effect that turns undead.

trait.name: Undead Nature
entries: The lich doesn’t require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Undead Resilience
entries: The lich is immune to poison damage, to exhaustion, and to the poisoned condition.

trait.name: Versatile Artist
entries: At the end of each long rest, the lich chooses one form of artistic expression, such as song, poetry, dance, fashion, paint, or similar. Until it finishes a long rest, the lich has immunity to one type of damage, which is associated with its artistic expression. For example, a lich expressing art through song or poetry has immunity to thunder damage, a lich expressing art through fashion has immunity to slashing damage, and a lich expressing art through paint has immunity to acid damage. This trait can’t give the lich immunity to force, psychic, or radiant damage.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The virtuoso lich uses Corrupted Art. It then makes three Artistic Flourish attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Spellcasting.

action.name: Artistic Flourish
entries: Melee or Ranged Spell Attack: +9 to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 60 ft., one target. Hit: 18 (3d8 + 5) damage of the type chosen with the Versatile Artist trait plus 9 (2d8) force damage.

action.name: Call Muse
entries: The lich chooses one Beast or Humanoid it can see within 30 feet of it to be its muse. The target must succeed on a DC 17 WIS save or become the lich’s muse and be charmed by the lich for 1 minute. While charmed, the muse has a speed of 0 and is incapacitated as it watches or listens to the lich’s artistic expression. The muse can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. If a creature’s save is successful or the effect ends for it, the creature is immune to the lich’s Call Muse for the next 24 hours. If the muse suffers harm from the lich, it is no longer charmed. The lich can have only one muse at a time. If it charms another, the effect on the previous muse ends. If the lich is within 30 feet of its muse and can see its muse, the lich has advantage on its first Artistic Flourish attack each round against a creature that isn’t its muse.

action.name: Corrupted Art
entries: The lich hums a discordant melody, paints a crumbling symbol of death in the air, performs a reality-bending pirouette, or emulates some other expression of corrupted or twisted art and targets one creature it can see, other than itself, within 60 feet. This action’s effects change, depending on if the target is Undead.
• Non-Undead. The target must make a DC 17 WIS save. On
a failure, a creature is blinded, charmed, or deafened (the lich’s choice) until the end of its next turn. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn’t incapacitated.
• Undead. The target regains 18 (4d8) HP . Healing that
exceeds the target’s HP maximum becomes temporary HP .

action.name: Spellcasting
entries: The lich casts one of the following spells, using CHA as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 17).
At will: disguise self, mage hand, message, prestidigitation
3/day each: charm, hideous laughter, hold, invisibility
(self only)
2/day each: dispel magic, fear, dimension door
1/day each: irresistible dance, programmed illusion, seeming

legendary.name: Text
entries: The virtuoso lich can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature’s turn. The lich regains spent legendary actions at the start of its turn.

legendary.name: Call Muse
entries: The lich uses Call Muse.

legendary.name: Move
entries: The lich moves up to its speed without provoking opportunity attacks.

legendary.name: Cast a Spell (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The lich uses Spellcasting.

legendary.name: Unrestrained Art (Costs 3 Actions)
entries: The lich unleashes the full force of its artistic talents. Each creature with 15 feet of the lich must make a DC 17 DEX save. On a failure, a creature takes 9 (2d8) damage of the type chosen with the Versatile Artist trait and is knocked prone. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn’t knocked prone. Living Colossus This immense statue towers above the trees, regal and powerful, its face serious and determined. As runes light up across it, the colossal legs begin to move, and the ground quakes with its every step.

legendary.name: Symbolic Monument
entries: A living colossus is a gigantic statue depicting a powerful or historically significant individual. Such a monument becomes a powerful symbol, allowing the spirit of the commemorated individual to animate the monument.

legendary.name: Varied Origins
entries: T o create a living colossus, first the stone must be carved and then clerics or wizards perform a ritual to draw the willing spirit into its monument. In some cases, a spirit spontaneously inhabits its monument because of a great need or the oaths the individual had in life.

monster.name: Living Colossus
languages: any languages the animating spirit knew in life

trait.name: Construct Nature
entries: The living colossus doesn’t require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Construct Resilience
entries: The living colossus is immune to poison and psychic damage, and it is immune to exhaustion and the charmed, frightened, paralyzed, petrified, and poisoned conditions.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the living colossus remains motionless, it is indistinguishable from a normal statue or monument of the individual it represents.

trait.name: Immutable Form
entries: The living colossus is immune to any spell or effect that would alter its form.

trait.name: Siege Monster
entries: The living colossus deals double damage to objects and structures.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The living colossus makes three Slam attacks, or it makes four Spirit Blast attacks.

action.name: Slam
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +15 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 32 (4d10 + 10) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Spirit Blast
entries: Ranged Spell Attack: +11 to hit, range 120 ft., one target. Hit: 24 (4d8 + 6) psychic damage.

action.name: Thunderous Clap (Recharge 5–6)
entries: The living colossus slams its hands together, creating a thunderous wave in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a DC 18 CON save, taking 59 (17d6) thunder damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

bonus.name: Quake
entries: The living colossus strikes the ground with its foot. Each creature within 30 feet of the living colossus must succeed on a DC 18 STR save or be knocked prone and have the air knocked from its lungs, if it is a breathing creature, until the start of the living colossus’s next turn. A creature with the air knocked from its lungs can’t speak or cast spells with verbal components. If a creature fails the save by 5 or more, it is also stunned until the start of the living colossus’s next turn.

reaction.name: Bolstering Spirit
entries: When a friendly creature the living colossus can see within 60 feet of it, other than itself, takes damage, the living colossus can share some of the life force of the spirit animating it with that friendly creature. The target gains 14 (4d6) temporary HP for 1 minute. If the target would take enough damage to reduce it to 0 HP , it instead regains 14 (4d8) HP . Lizardfolk The muscular reptilian humanoid tastes the air with a forked tongue, its rumbling hiss as much a warning as its sharp claws and weapons.

reaction.name: Cold-Blooded Humanoids
entries: Lizardfolk are slightly larger and more powerfully built than humans. Their cold-blooded nature allows them to thrive in tropical and temperate environments, though most live in swamps. A few scattered groups of lizardfolk carve out small territories in the verdant river deltas near salt marshes, jungles, or deserts.

reaction.name: Fallen Civilization
entries: Lizardfolk believe that they ruled portions of the world long ago. Though their society today is less advanced, they take great pride in stories of their former greatness, and their communities are often found clinging to the crumbling ruins of their ancestors.

reaction.name: Omnivorous Pragmatists
entries: The lizardfolk are eminently practical creatures, which leads some humanoids to believe lizardfolk are unemotional. However, lizardfolk would say that they simply temper their feelings with pragmatism. Even their diet emphasizes practical or available foods rather than what might be tastiest.

reaction.name: Swamp Magic
entries: Lizardfolk magic is primordial and taps into their connection with swamps, rainforests, and the creatures found there. Lizardfolk shamans coat their weapons in frog poison and snake venom and channel that poison into deadly magical blasts. When in danger, they can also coat their bodies in poison and bright paints to discourage would-be attackers or predators.

reaction.name: Inspiring Leaders
entries: Lizardfolk are even pragmatic when choosing their leaders. Most lizardfolk clans are led by a council of elder lizardfolk rulers. Future lizardfolk rulers are chosen when lizardfolk youth come of age. Y outh who inspire, encourage, and defend their kin, often risking their own lives for their fellows, are swiftly promoted to positions of responsibility and trained by elders, a current shaman, or retired rulers. Once trained, these rulers lead groups of lizardfolk hunters, scouts, emissaries, and defenders.

trait.name: Heightened Smell and Taste
entries: The lizardfolk’s Perception is 18 when perceiving by smell or taste.

trait.name: Hold Breath
entries: The lizardfolk can hold its breath for 15 minutes.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The lizardfolk makes one Bite attack and one Heavy Club attack, or it makes two Javelin attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) piercing damage.

action.name: Heavy Club
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Javelin
entries: Melee or Ranged Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 30/120 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) piercing damage.

reaction.name: Shoving Counter
entries: If a creature misses the lizardfolk with an attack and the lizardfolk is wielding a shield, the lizardfolk can shove its shield into the creature. The attacker must succeed on a DC 12 STR save or be pushed up to 10 feet away from the lizardfolk and knocked prone.

reaction.name: Spellcasting
entries: The lizardfolk shaman casts one of the following spells, using WIS as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 13).
At will: druidcraft, guidance, mending
2/day each: cure wounds, entangle, grease
1/day: ray of enfeeblement

bonus.name: Poisoned Body (32 HP or Fewer)
entries: The shaman coats itself in poison until the start of its next turn. It takes 3 (1d6) poison damage. In addition, the next time a creature hits the shaman with a melee attack while within 5 feet of the shaman before the start of the shaman’s next turn, the attacker takes 7 (2d6) poison damage.

trait.name: Heightened Smell and Taste
entries: The lizardfolk’s Perception is 20 when perceiving by smell or taste.

trait.name: Hold Breath
entries: The lizardfolk shaman can hold its breath for 15 minutes.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The lizardfolk makes one Bite attack and one Ritual Staff attack, or it makes two Swamp Bolt attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) piercing damage.

action.name: Ritual Staff
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d8 + 2) bludgeoning damage plus 7 (2d6) poison damage.

action.name: Swamp Bolt
entries: Ranged Spell Attack: +5 to hit, range 60 ft., one target. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) poison damage, and the target must succeed on a DC 13 CON save or be poisoned until the end of its next turn.

trait.name: Brutal
entries: A melee weapon deals one extra die of its damage when the lizardfolk ruler hits with it (included in the attack).

trait.name: Heightened Smell and Taste
entries: The lizardfolk’s Perception is 17 when perceiving by smell or taste.

trait.name: Hold Breath
entries: The lizardfolk ruler can hold its breath for 15 minutes.

trait.name: Unshakable Command
entries: Each friendly creature within 30 feet of the lizardfolk ruler can’t be charmed or frightened while the ruler isn’t incapacitated.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The lizardfolk ruler makes two Bite attacks and one Greataxe attack, or it makes three Javelin attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) piercing damage.

action.name: Greataxe
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 16 (2d12 + 3) slashing damage.

action.name: Javelin
entries: Melee or Ranged Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 30/120 ft., one target. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) piercing damage in melee or 6 (1d6 + 3) piercing damage at range.

bonus.name: Text
entries: Leader’s Challenge. The ruler hisses out a challenge or a threat (the ruler’s choice) toward one creature it can see within 30 feet of it. The target must make a DC 13 CHA save. If the target fails the save against a challenge, the target has disadvantage on attack rolls against any creature other than the ruler until the start of the ruler’s next turn. If the target fails the save against a threat, the ruler has advantage on the next attack roll it makes against the target before the start of its next turn.

reaction.name: Inspiring Hiss
entries: When a friendly lizardfolk the ruler can see within 30 feet of it is hit by an attack, the ruler can hiss out inspiring words to encourage the lizardfolk to keep going. The target gains 5 (2d4) temporary HP for 1 minute. CHAMELEON LIZARDFOLK Some lizardfolk have bodies similar to chameleons. They can change the hue of their scales to match their surroundings, and they have extremely long tongues for catching insects and other small prey. These lizardfolk have the Adaptive Camouflage trait and the Tongue Trip bonus action.

reaction.name: Adaptive Camouflage
entries: If the lizardfolk spends at least 1 minute in a particular type of terrain, such as swamp, forest, or underwater, its Stealth increases by 5 while in that terrain.

reaction.name: Tongue Trip
entries: The lizardfolk lashes out its tongue at a creature it can see within 15 feet of it. The target must succeed on a STR save or be knocked prone. The save DC equals the save DC of the lizardfolk’s other features, such as Shoving Counter, Spellcasting, or Leader’s Challenge. Lycanthropes A rat scurries through the sewers, climbs up a pipe, and squeezes out of a drain into an alley. Its body suddenly pops and cracks as it grows and changes into a scrawny, unkempt man.

reaction.name: Shapechanging Humanoids
entries: Lycanthropes can change their humanoid bodies into the forms of beasts or a hybrid of the two. Many variants exist, including rats, bears, boars, and wolves. While in humanoid form, they often take on personality aspects inherent in their bestial sides. While in animal form, they are larger than most others of their kind and demonstrate great cunning and intelligence.

reaction.name: Cursed Origins
entries: Lycanthropes are humanoids suffering an ancient curse, which made the first lycanthropes prone to uncontrolled rages and transformed them into beasts, often without remembering the event. These rages were so terrifying—for the victims and the cursed—that the legends remain what most people know of lycanthropes. Newly cursed lycanthropes temporarily suffer the same rages, further supporting the longevity of such legends. Some lycanthropes embrace their terrifying reputation, and others seek to simply live out their lives in peace. A few live among other humanoids, hiding their true selves, but most live with other lycanthropes in distant settlements.

reaction.name: Controlled Curse
entries: Most lycanthropes can control their shapechanging, except in times of extreme duress, and retain their memories and personalities while transformed. Cursed lycanthropes, those who became lycanthropes after being bitten, enter uncontrolled rages for only the first few months after being cursed. They eventually learn to control their bestial side. Natural-born lycanthropes (those with at least one lycanthrope parent) don’t enter uncontrolled rages from their lycanthropy.

reaction.name: Painful Transformation
entries: Lycanthropy does more than just provoke the savagery of an enraged beast; the transformation itself is painful. Bones and joints crack and pop, muscles stretch and rend, and claws and snouts burst from flesh. Even natural-born lycanthropes aren’t exempt from the pain of transformation. As a result, most lycanthropes have a high tolerance for pain; however, silver overrides much of this tolerance, making it a universal weapon against dangerous lycanthropes. SPECIFIC LYCANTHROPES The most common types of lycanthropes are werebears, wereboars, wererats, weretigers, and werewolves. These descriptions apply mostly to natural-born lycanthropes. Those cursed with lycanthropy through a bite retain the personalities and ideals they had before becoming cursed.

reaction.name: Werebears
entries: Werebears live in small, tight-knit family units in the forests, mountains, and wild places of the world. Of all the lycanthropes, werebears have the closest connection to nature and the natural world, and many werebears are kindly creatures that protect nature, tend the wounds of animals, and preserve the balance by culling overpopulations. Newly cursed werebears often become inordinately protective of nature, their homes, and their families, lashing out at the slightest perceived threats.

reaction.name: Wereboars
entries: Wereboars live in large family groups in forests, grasslands, and foothills. They enjoy the simple pleasures in life, like tearing into a farmer’s field of vegetables or standing firm against a pack of wolves. Wereboars can anger quickly, especially when challenged. Wereboars tend to tear up fields and forest floors in search of food, annoying fey and farmers equally. They have a great fondness for ale and feasting and for taking apart hunting parties that expect to spear normal boar for sport.

reaction.name: Wererats
entries: Wererats live in and around cities and towns. Of all the lycanthropes, they are the most intermingled with humanoids, often living as sailors, thieves, musicians, and scouts. When they are part of a family group, they reveal their true nature only when initiating an individual into the family by sharing the wererat curse. Newly cursed wererats often experience uncontrolled fits of kleptomania. CURSE OF L YCANTHROPY When a lycanthrope bites a Humanoid or otherwise passes its saliva into a Humanoid’s wound, it can spread the curse of its lycanthropy to the Humanoid. For the first 30 days after becoming infected, the Humanoid experiences dreams and nightmares related to the Beast associated with its curse, such as bear or wolf. During this time, the curse can be cured with the remove curse spell or similar magic. After the 30 days has passed, the Humanoid undergoes its first transformation in its sleep, and it spends the evening without control of its body and acting in a way appropriate to a newly cursed member of its type of lycanthropy, as defined in the lycanthrope’s lore. Once the first transformation takes place, the creature can no longer be cured of lycanthropy, except by a wish spell or similar magic. A newly cursed Humanoid continues transforming each night and remembers only fragments of these events. Every 7 days, it can repeat the save it made when it was first cursed. When it succeeds on this save three times, it gains full control of its transformation, retains its memories while transformed, and no longer has to make this save, becoming a true lycanthrope.

reaction.name: Weretigers
entries: Weretigers live solitary lives in forests and grasslands. They take a mate long enough to raise their young, then all go their separate ways. The rarest and most elusive of lycanthropes, weretigers are the most bestial of their kind, spending more time in beast form than in humanoid shape. Newly cursed weretigers often hunt and kill for weeks, as their powerful hunger claws at them.

reaction.name: Werewolves
entries: Extremely pack-oriented, hereditary werewolves live in family groups led by a pair of grandparents or great-grandparents. These groups live nomadically, hunting and gathering as they follow migrating game through forests and foothills. Lone werewolves are also quite common, especially among those who gain the curse through a bite, and often seek to create their own packs by spreading the curse. Werewolf lycanthropy is the most widespread and widely known, and to this day, the average person believes there is only one form of lycanthropy: werewolf.

entries: The werebear’s Perception is 19 when perceiving by smell.

action.name: Multiattack

action.name: Greataxe (Humanoid or Hybrid Form Only)

bonus.name: Change Shape

Its statistics, other than its size, speed, and AC, are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn’t transformed. It reverts to its true form if it dies.

bonus.name: Pin
entries: One creature grappled by the werebear is knocked prone, and it is restrained until the grapple ends.

monster.name: Wereboar

trait.name: Relentless (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)

entries: The wereboar makes three Maul or Slam attacks. It can replace one attack with a Tusks attack, provided it is in the appropriate form. If two Maul or Slam attacks hit one creature, the target must succeed on a DC 13 STR save or be knocked prone.

action.name: Slam (Boar or Hybrid Form Only)

action.name: Tusks (Boar or Hybrid Form Only)

bonus.name: Challenging Snort (Boar or Hybrid Form Only)
entries: The wereboar snorts and stomps its feet at a creature it can see within 30 feet of it. The target must succeed on a DC 13 WIS save or be frightened until the end of its next turn and have disadvantage on the next save it makes against being knocked prone by the wereboar.

bonus.name: Change Shape
entries: The wereboar transforms into a Medum or Large boar, a Medium boar-humanoid hybrid, or back into its true form, which is Humanoid. Its statistics, other than its size, speed, and AC, are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn’t transformed. It reverts to its true form if it dies.

Its statistics, other than its size and speed, are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn’t transformed. It reverts to its true form if it dies.

bonus.name: Bleed Prey
entries: The weretiger chooses a creature it can see within 30 feet of it to be its prey until the prey dies, until the weretiger dies, or until the weretiger uses this bonus action on another creature. While within 60 feet of its prey, the weretiger can pinpoint its prey’s location. In addition, if the weretiger hits the prey with at least two melee weapon attacks, the weretiger rends the prey’s flesh, and the prey must succeed on a DC 13 CON save or lose 7 (2d6) HP at the start of its next turn, as the wound bleeds excessively.

bonus.name: Change Shape

Its statistics, other than its size and speed, are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn’t transformed. It reverts to its true form if it dies.

entries: The werewolf stares down and growls at one creature it can see within 30 feet of it. The target must succeed on a DC 13 WIS FORCED TRANSFORMATION AND CONSENT When a Humanoid first transforms into a lycanthrope and until it becomes a true lycanthrope, as defined in the Curse of Lycanthropy sidebar, it has no control over itself while transformed, and each transformation lasts for approximately the duration of a long rest. Whether through charm, illusion, or lycanthropy, losing autonomy over a character can feel like a personal invasion. As a GM, if you plan to include lycanthropy, talk with your players ahead of time about how the rules around this curse will play out. You will take control of the character for a time, and it might result in the character doing things that the player wouldn't choose. However, the player must still deal with the consequences of the character's in-game actions while under your control. For more information about consent and how to have these conversations with your players, see Safety Tools in the introduction of the Player’s Guide. Other options include leaving lycanthropy out of your game, making the curse require extra effort to obtain, or redefining lycanthropy so that players retain control over their characters but suffer some other penalty. Magmin This small creature’s skin is a shell of barely cooled magma. Waves of heat rise from it as plumes of fire burn in its eyes and mouth.

bonus.name: Miniature Volcanoes
entries: Native to the Elemental Planes, magmin live on the churning, tumultuous border between the planes of earth and fire. They feed on fire-blasted rock and carve channels in the earth for lava to flow, much like a farmer might irrigate crops. On the Material Plane, they inhabit volcanic vents or inactive volcanoes, feasting on the volcanic rock, often channeling the flow of lava to expand a volcano’s reach. When slain, they erupt in a blast of lava.

bonus.name: Easy to Summon
entries: Of all the elementals, magmin are the easiest to summon, and many spellcasters call magmin to their sides for protection, for heating alchemical reactions or firing arcane kilns and forges, or for testing their powers of conjuration. A blend of calm earth and temperamental fire, magmin are generally complacent when summoned, especially for short-term tasks. However, they are quick to anger and turn on their summoners if mistreated or kept from their homes for longer than a week or two. Many spellcasters and alchemists have returned to find their laboratories or notebooks turned to ashes by unhappy magmin leaving in a blaze of fire.

trait.name: Death Burst
entries: When the magmin dies, it collapses into a Tiny cinder cone. On initiative count 20 after the magmin dies, the cinder cone erupts in a burst of fire and lava. Each creature within 10 feet of the cone must make a DC 11 DEX save, taking 7 (2d6) fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Flammable objects that aren’t being worn or carried in that area are ignited.

trait.name: Hardened Magma
entries: While the magmin isn’t ablaze, it is resistant to piercing and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks, and it is vulnerable to thunder damage.

trait.name: Molten Body
entries: While the magmin is ablaze, each creature that hits it with a melee attack while within 5 feet of it takes 3 (1d6) fire damage.

action.name: Burning Slam
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 4 (1d4 + 2) bludgeoning damage plus 3 (1d6) fire damage.

bonus.name: Ignited Illumination
entries: The magmin sets itself ablaze or extinguishes its flames. While ablaze, the magmin sheds bright light in a 10-foot radius and dim light for an additional 10 feet. Manticore A creature with the wings of a bat, the body of a lion, a spiked tail, and a human head stalks through the grass, crying plaintively.

bonus.name: Hunters of the Wilds
entries: Manticores are fierce, stalking carnivores. Typically solitary hunters, they sometimes band together in a pack to take down larger prey. Rarer still, a particularly powerful or clever manticore takes up a tribe of smaller or younger manticores and may even demand tribute from local humanoids.

bonus.name: Humanoid Eaters
entries: Manticores prefer to prey upon humanoids and generally intelligent creatures. They can mimic voices, luring hapless victims into an ambush. They feast upon a few victims, then flee the region before locals start to band together against them.

bonus.name: Retribution of Nature
entries: Manticores hunt in areas where humanoids are plentiful or areas where humanoids have despoiled nature. By culling humanoids in an area, they often help nature recover from the damage. Some manticores lean into this idea of being nature’s retribution and threaten “nature’s wrath” if locals don’t give them tribute.

trait.name: Mimicry
entries: The manticore can mimic Humanoid voices. A creature that hears the sounds can tell they are imitations with a successful DC 13 WIS (Insight) check.

trait.name: Monstrosity Resilience
entries: The manticore is resistant to exhaustion and to the frightened condition.

trait.name: Tail Spike Regrowth
entries: The manticore has twenty-four tail spikes. Used spikes regrow when the manticore finishes a long rest.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The manticore makes one Bite attack and two Claw attacks, or it makes two Tail Spike attacks. If both Tail Spike attacks hit one creature, the target must succeed on a DC 13 CON save or be poisoned until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) piercing damage.

action.name: Claw
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) slashing damage.

action.name: Tail Spike
entries: Ranged Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, range 100/200 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) piercing damage plus 5 (2d4) poison damage.

reaction.name: Spiky Retaliation
entries: When a creature the manticore can see hits it with a melee attack while within 5 feet of it, the manticore can jab a spike into the attacker. The attacker must succeed on a DC 13 DEX save or take 5 (2d4) poison damage. VARIANT: WINGLESS MANTICORE Many manticores, especially those inhabiting grasslands, don’t have wings, stalking prey through tall grasses rather than from the air. Such a manticore doesn’t have a flying speed, and it gains the following trait:

reaction.name: Pounce
entries: If the manticore moves at least 15 feet straight toward a creature and then hits it with a Claw attack on the same turn, that target must succeed on a DC 13 STR save or be knocked prone. If the target is prone, the manticore can make one Bite attack against it as a bonus action. Mechadrons A squat construct with a heavy body, thick limbs, and a central mechanical eye steadily digs a very precise trench, while a cylindrical construct with a single eye zips around the air above it.

reaction.name: Planar Constructs
entries: Mechadrons are living constructs that inhabit the outer planes of law and order. All mechadrons are manufactured by the mechadrons that came before. Each is crafted for a specific purpose, and mechadrons work in unison to accomplish seemingly impossible tasks.

reaction.name: We of One
entries: All mechadrons refer to themselves as “we” and seem intrinsically drawn to one another. No one knows if mechadrons even contain a sense of self or desire outside of “The Plan,” a goal they all serve.

reaction.name: Machine Speech Relay
entries: A mechadron can relay communications between other mechadrons using a specialized, high-pitched dialect of Machine Speech. This helps larger mechadrons coordinate attacks, marches, ambushes, or looting operations. Mechadrons can also relay Machine Speech with one another in line of sight using nearly imperceptible eye movements. They transfer vast amounts of information in seconds this way. Sometimes, parts of their Machine Speech break into Common, leaving listeners with tidbits of their conversation.

reaction.name: Reconfiguration
entries: Mechadrons don’t evolve, grow, or change on their own. However, they can combine with a few hours of work, reconfiguring themselves for greater effectiveness and potency. For instance, a solodron that combines with a dupladron becomes a tripladron. Through reconfiguration, mechadrons drastically change their demeanor, skill, and purpose. Through great effort and planning, a mechadron can reconfigure itself back into its original components, though this is rare.

reaction.name: Solodron
entries: The solodron is a fast-flying surveyor and scout. From a mile away, it can judge measurements and distances to within a fraction of an inch, or one thousandth of a quantant, the universal unit of measure that all mechadrons use, without offering any conversion for other creatures. Roughly speaking, a quantant is about 1.5 feet.

reaction.name: Dupladron
entries: Heavily crafted, the dupladron tirelessly works to reshape its environment in whatever way the Plan instructs. The dupladron relies on solodron scouts to ensure precise positioning of each structure. Capable of creating or demolishing sturdy structures in a few hours or days, solodrons can even dig trenches in minutes, providing shelter for themselves, companions, or plunder.

reaction.name: Tripladron
entries: Sturdy and weaponized, tripladrons function as field troops and repairers, defending and repairing other mechadrons in the field. Their bodies contain a variety of arms tipped with different implements, such as grabbing pincers, drills, slicing blades, and small hammers, all of which can be used alternately for repairs or in defense of their fellow mechadrons.

reaction.name: Tetradron
entries: The strongest and most intelligent of the mechadrons, the tetradron’s mechanical wings and helmet-like head make it look almost angelic beside other mechadrons. T etradrons are the core carriers of the Plan, and their precise leadership ensures each mechadron does its part to enact the Plan. T etradrons protect other mechadrons, especially from living creatures, but all mechadrons understand the importance of the tetradrons’ role, sacrificing themselves to protect the carriers of the Plan at all costs. If the worst should come to pass and a tetradron is destroyed, the surviving mechadrons gather what remains of the tetradron and reconfigure themselves into a new tetradron, absorbing the slain tetradron’s knowledge and continuing to enact the directives of the Plan.

trait.name: Construct Nature
entries: The solodron doesn’t require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Construct Resilience
entries: The solodron is immune to poison and psychic damage, and it is immune to exhaustion and the charmed, frightened, paralyzed, petrified, and poisoned conditions.

trait.name: Unerring Mind
entries: The solodron can perfectly recall information it has seen or heard in the past 90 days.

action.name: Melee Armament
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +3 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 3 (1d4 + 1) bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage (the solodron’s choice).

action.name: Eye Beam
entries: Ranged Spell Attack: +4 to hit, range 120 ft., one target. Hit: 4 (1d4 + 2) force damage.

bonus.name: Machine Vigor
entries: The solodron ends one spell, magical effect, or condition affecting it.

trait.name: Construct Nature
entries: The dupladron doesn’t require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Construct Resilience
entries: The dupladron is immune to poison and psychic damage, and it is immune to exhaustion and the charmed, frightened, paralyzed, petrified, and poisoned conditions.

trait.name: Siege Monster
entries: The dupladron deals double damage to objects and structures.

trait.name: Unerring Mind
entries: The dupladron can perfectly recall information it has seen or heard in the past 90 days.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The dupladron makes two Melee Armament attacks.

action.name: Melee Armament
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +3 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (2d4 + 1) bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage (the dupladron’s choice).

action.name: Eye Beam
entries: Ranged Spell Attack: +4 to hit, range 120 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (2d4 + 2) force damage.

action.name: Stitch Together
entries: The dupladron casts the mending cantrip, requiring no material components and using INT as the spellcasting ability.

bonus.name: Machine Vigor
entries: The dupladron ends one spell, magical effect, or condition affecting it.

monster.name: Tripladron
resistNoteFromTraits: bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from

trait.name: Construct Nature
entries: The tripladron doesn’t require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Construct Resilience
entries: The tripladron is immune to poison and psychic damage, and it is immune to exhaustion and the charmed, frightened, paralyzed, petrified, and poisoned conditions. Three’s Vulnerability. When a creature makes a weapon attack against the tripladron and the roll on the die is a 3 or 13, the attack scores a critical hit against the tripladron, regardless of the attack total. When a creature’s weapon damage roll ends in a “3,” the tripladron isn’t resistant to the damage, regardless of the damage type.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The tripladron makes three Blade Arm or Launch Drill attacks. It can replace one Blade attack with a Grabber attack.

action.name: Blade Arm
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 12 (2d8 + 3) slashing damage.

action.name: Grabber
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) bludgeoning damage, and the target is grappled (escape DC 13) if it is a Medium or smaller creature. Until this grapple ends, the tripladron can’t use its Grabber attack.

action.name: Launch Drill
entries: Ranged Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, range 30/120 ft., one target. Hit: 11 (2d8 + 2) piercing damage.

bonus.name: Field Repair
entries: The tripladron repairs one Construct other than itself that it can see within 10 feet of it. The target regains 7 (2d6) HP and has disadvantage on attack rolls until the end of its next turn. A Construct can’t be restored to more than half its HP maximum from Field Repair.

bonus.name: Machine Vigor
entries: The tripladron ends one spell, magical effect, or condition affecting it.

trait.name: Construct Nature
entries: The tetradron doesn’t require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Construct Resilience
entries: The tetradron is immune to poison and psychic damage, and it is immune to exhaustion and the charmed, frightened, paralyzed, petrified, and poisoned conditions. Four’s Vulnerability. When a creature makes a weapon attack against the tetradron and the roll on the die is a 4 or 14, the attack scores a critical hit against the tetradron, regardless of the attack total. When a creature’s weapon damage roll ends in a “4,” the tetradron isn’t resistant to the damage, regardless of the damage type.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The tetradron makes four Metal Claw or Launch Wing Blade attacks. If at least two Metal Claw attacks hit one creature, the target is grappled (escape DC 15) if it is a Large or smaller creature. The tetradron can grapple only one creature at a time.

action.name: Metal Claw
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) piercing damage.

action.name: Launch Wing Blade
entries: Ranged Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, range 30/120 ft., one target. Hit: 11 (2d8 + 2) slashing damage.

action.name: Wing Slice (Recharge 5–6)
entries: The tetradron extends its wings and slices in a 15-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a DC 15 DEX save, taking 27 (6d8) slashing damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

action.name: Call Mechadrons (1/Day)
entries: The tetradron magically calls 2d6 solodrons, 2d4 dupladrons, or 1 tripladron. The called creatures arrive in 1d4 rounds, acting as allies of the tetradron and obeying its spoken commands. The Constructs remain for 1 hour, until the tetradron dies, or until the tetradron dismisses them as a bonus action.

bonus.name: Inject Sedative
entries: The tetradron injects one creature it is grappling that isn’t a Construct or Undead with a mild sedative. The target must succeed on a DC 15 CON save or be incapacitated for 1 minute. The incapacitated creature can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

bonus.name: Machine Vigor
entries: The tetradron ends one spell, magical effect, or condition affecting it.

reaction.name: Protect the Plan
entries: When a creature the tetradron can see targets it with an attack, it can choose for a mechadron within 5 feet of it to become the new target. Medusa The woman suddenly pulls back the hood of her cloak to reveal hair formed of twisting serpents. Her eyes flash with a golden light, and the snakes begin to hiss.

reaction.name: Snake-Haired Connoisseurs
entries: Medusas are human-like creatures with snakes sprouting from their heads in place of hair. They are clever, charismatic, and have a fierce love of art, music, and finery. Medusas are most known for their deadly gaze, which can turn flesh into stone. Indeed, a few have posed as talented sculptors, though these restrict their work to animal subjects.

reaction.name: Hidden Enclaves
entries: Most medusas dwell in remote areas, where their gaze doesn’t sow chaos. They carve elaborate sculptures, play music on harps or flutes, and weave colorful clothes. They are also drawn to art made by others. Many otherwise reclusive medusas risk exposure by sneaking into cities or villages to observe and enjoy such art.

reaction.name: Criminal Masterminds
entries: Some medusas use their natural charm, deadly gaze, and near-agelessness to create and run spy networks, criminal organizations, or information brokerages in major cities. They remain masked and hooded when interacting with clients or agents, and they use names or titles meant to impress, such as Matron of Mysteries, Starlit Shadow, or Master of Ashes.

trait.name: Monstrosity Resilience
entries: The medusa is resistant to exhaustion and to the frightened condition.

trait.name: Petrifying Gaze
entries: When a creature that can see the medusa’s eyes starts its turn within 30 feet of the medusa, the medusa can force it to make a DC 14 CON save if the medusa isn’t incapacitated and can see the creature. If the save fails by 5 or more, the creature is instantly petrified. Otherwise, a creature that fails the save begins to turn to stone and is restrained. The restrained creature must repeat the save at the end of its next turn, becoming petrified on a failure or ending the effect on itself on a success. The petrification lasts until the creature is freed by the greater restoration spell or other magic. Unless surprised, a creature can avert its eyes to avoid the save at the start of its turn. If the creature does so, it can’t see the medusa until the start of its next turn, when it can avert its eyes again. If the creature looks at the medusa in the meantime, it must immediately make the save. If the medusa sees itself reflected on a polished surface within 30 feet of it and in an area of bright light, the medusa, due to its curse, is affected by its own gaze. A medusa isn’t resistant to its own gaze.

trait.name: Snake Friend
entries: The medusa can communicate with snakes as if they shared a language, and snakes can’t be petrified by the medusa’s Petrifying Gaze.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The medusa makes one Snake Hair attack and two Shortsword attacks, or it makes three Longbow attacks.

action.name: Snake Hair
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d4 + 4) piercing damage plus 14 (4d6) poison damage.

action.name: Shortsword
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d6 + 4) piercing damage plus 7 (2d6) poison damage.

action.name: Longbow
entries: Ranged Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, range 150/600 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (1d8 + 4) piercing damage plus 7 (2d6) poison damage.

reaction.name: Interruptive Hiss
entries: When a creature the medusa can see targets it with an attack, the medusa’s snakes can suddenly rise and hiss at the attacker. The attacker must succeed on a DC 14 WIS save or fail the attack roll. Mephits The clouds of dust and steam cackle as they form into two figures, each with sharply pointed ears, long noses, and clawed fingers and toes.

reaction.name: Mischievous Elementals
entries: Capricious and hopelessly intrusive, mephits are small elemental creatures native to the various Elemental Planes. A group of mephits is called a “pest,” and indeed, many creatures that regularly interact with mephits think the term might be much too kind. They tend to be chatty, overeager creatures that leap before looking, and they rarely understand the concepts of manners or belongings. Mephits are much more interested in having fun—if that makes someone else sad, it’s not their problem.

reaction.name: Questionable Origins
entries: The inhabitants of the Elemental Planes believe mephits are either a natural phenomenon like elemental roaches or mice or fey spirits trapped in elemental bodies. None of the inhabitants of the Elemental Planes want to claim the mephits as their friends or creations, a fact that doesn’t bother mephits.

reaction.name: Summoned Familiars
entries: Mephits are commonly summoned by spellcasters needing a mixture of elements to complete alchemical processes or an assistant with a touch more magic or elemental power than a typical familiar. Some mischievous spellcasters feel a kinship with mephits and take a mephit as a familiar. Such spellcasters often encourage pranks or join in on the mephit’s fun, and the bond between two such tricksters can be lifelong and nigh-unbreakable.

reaction.name: Explosive Deaths
entries: Each mephit fuses two different elements, leaving its body somewhat unstable. When a mephit is slain, this unstable mixture explodes, as the elements separate and return to their raw states.

reaction.name: Various Elements
entries: Mephits can be comprised of any two elements, but the four most common mephits are dust (air and earth), ice (air and water), magma (earth and fire), and steam (fire and water). All mephits share many personality traits, but the elements comprising a mephit subtly affect its personality, causing it to exhibit mild traits related to its component elements. For example, magma mephits tend to be quicker to anger than other mephits, and dust mephits go with the flow of events around them more often than other mephits.

trait.name: Death Burst
entries: When the mephit dies, it explodes in a burst of dust. Each creature within 5 feet of it must succeed on a DC 11 DEX save or be blinded for 1 minute. A blinded creature can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the mephit remains motionless and hovering, it is indistinguishable from an ordinary cloud of dust.

action.name: Claws
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 4 (1d4 + 2) slashing damage, and the target loses 2 (1d4) HP at the start of its next turn, as sand and grit irritate the wound.

action.name: Dust Blast
entries: Ranged Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, range 20/60 ft., one target. Hit: 4 (1d4 + 2) bludgeoning damage, and the target loses 2 (1d4) HP at the start of its next turn, as sand and grit irritate the wound.

action.name: Blinding Breath (Recharge 6)
entries: The mephit exhales blinding dust in a 15-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a DC 11 DEX save. On a failure, a creature takes 5 (2d4) bludgeoning damage and is blinded for 1 minute. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn’t blinded. A blinded creature can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

bonus.name: Text
entries: Sandman’s Call (1/Day). The mephit casts the sleep spell (spell save DC 11), requiring no material components and using WIS as the spellcasting ability.

trait.name: Death Burst
entries: When the mephit dies, it explodes in a burst of viscous ice. Each creature within 5 feet of it must succeed on a DC 11 DEX save or be restrained by ice for 1 minute. A creature, including the restrained creature, can take its action to free the restrained creature by succeeding on a DC 11 STR check. The ice melts immediately if the restrained creature takes fire damage, freeing the creature.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the mephit remains motionless, it is indistinguishable from an ordinary shard of ice.

action.name: Claws
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 4 (1d4 + 2) slashing damage plus 3 (1d6) cold damage.

action.name: Ice Shard
entries: Ranged Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, range 20/60 ft., one target. Hit: 4 (1d4 + 2) piercing damage plus 3 (1d6) cold damage.

action.name: Frost Breath (Recharge 6)
entries: The mephit exhales freezing fog in a 15-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a DC 11 CON save. On a failure, a creature takes 7 (2d6) cold damage and is restrained by ice until the end of its next turn. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn’t restrained.

bonus.name: Cloud of Snow (1/Day)
entries: The mephit casts the fog cloud spell, requiring no material components and using CHA as the spellcasting ability.

trait.name: Death Burst
entries: When the mephit dies, it explodes in a burst of lava. Each creature within 5 feet of it must succeed on a DC 11 DEX save or be pushed up to 10 feet away from the mephit, knocked prone, and catch on fire. Until a creature takes an action to douse the fire, the creature on fire takes 3 (1d6) fire damage at the start of each of its turns.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the mephit remains motionless, it is indistinguishable from an ordinary mound of molten rock.

action.name: Claws
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 4 (1d4 + 2) slashing damage plus 3 (1d6) fire damage.

action.name: Lob Magma
entries: Ranged Weapon Attack: +2 to hit, range 20/60 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (2d6) fire damage.

action.name: Pyroclastic Burst (Recharge 6)
entries: The mephit exhales an explosive blast of lava in a 15-foot cone. Each creature in the area must make a DC 11 STR save. On a failure, a creature takes 7 (2d6) fire damage and is pushed up to

bonus.name: Pyrotechnic Display (1/Day)
