entries: The star crow learns some of the attached target’s memories, and the attached target experiences a rapid sequence of memories from other creatures the star crow has encountered. The target takes 5 (2d4) psychic damage and must succeed on a DC 13 CHA save or be stunned until the end of its next turn.

bonus.name: Blinding Burst (Recharge 5–6)
entries: The star crow dims then suddenly bursts with blinding light. Each creature within 30 feet of the star crow must succeed on a DC 13 CON save or be blinded until the end of its next turn. VARIANT: SHADOW STIRGE Stirges nest in dark places, and on occasion, those nests lie in areas where the boundary between the Material Plane and Plane of Shadow is thin. The power of that plane seeps into the nest, infusing the larva with shadow. A murky aura surrounds these stirges, known as shadow stirges. While attached to a creature, a shadow stirge can’t attack, and at the start of each of the stirge’s turns, the target’s STR score is reduced by 1d4 instead of losing HP due to blood loss. In addition, a shadow stirge has the following bonus action:

bonus.name: Shadow Stealth
entries: The shadow stirge takes the Hide action. It can use this bonus action only while in dim light or darkness. Stirge A small, bat-winged insectoid descends, grasping with claw-like legs and stabbing with a long, sharp proboscis.

bonus.name: Mammalian Insect
entries: About the size of a house cat, a stirge is a strange amalgam of mammal and insect. The stirge’s body is covered in a coarse, sparse hair, and its multi-jointed legs extend from its sides like a pair of claws. Two pairs of round, black eyes adorn its wedge-shaped head, which ends in a long, flexible proboscis with a needle-like bone tip. It flits through the air on leathery wings, halting only long enough to drink its fill from a creature.

bonus.name: Hematophagous
entries: Stirges feed on the blood of other creatures to survive. They use their claw-like legs to latch onto a victim, then stab their proboscises into their victims to feast. They typically hunt in large groups, allowing them to easily overwhelm and subdue prey while sustaining the colony. Once a stirge has drunk its fill, it detaches from the victim and returns to its nest. Dark Nests. Stirges prefer to create nests in dark, quiet locations, such as abandoned buildings, hillside caves, or even the dimly lit undergrowth of a rainforest. These nests can include dozens to hundreds of the creatures and their eggs, which require regular injections of blood through the soft shell to remain viable.

action.name: Blood Drain
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. Hit: 4 (1d4 + 2) piercing damage, and the stirge attaches to the target. While attached, the stirge can’t attack, and at the start of each of the stirge’s turns, the target loses 4 (1d4 + 2) HP due to blood loss. The attached stirge moves with the target whenever the target moves, requiring none of the stirge’s movement. If can detach itself by spending 5 feet of its movement on its turn. It does so after it drains 8 HP of blood from the target or the target dies. A creature, including the target, can use its action to detach the stirge.

reaction.name: Reactive Wiggle
entries: When the stirge is the target of an attack from a creature within 5 feet of it while it is attached to a creature, the stirge can wiggle, exposing the flesh where it is attached. The attacker must succeed on a DC 12 DEX save or the attack targets the creature to which the stirge is attached instead of the stirge. Succubus The sultry humanoid steps forward, its humanoid features slowly melting away to reveal red, leathery wings, curling horns, black claws, and red, glowing eyes.

reaction.name: Fiendish Tempters
entries: Succubi are preeminent tempters, working against the resolve of mortal creatures. Like most fiends, they seek to corrupt mortal souls to eventually use as currency in the Lower Planes. Succubi corrupt mortals, typically through temptations of the heart or flesh, by convincing them to seek their deepest desires without thought to the consequences.

reaction.name: Mediating Fiends
entries: Neither demon nor devil, and subsisting on mortal passion rather than mortal souls, the succubus holds a unique place among the fiends of the Lower Planes. Able to move freely among demons and devils, succubi are often employed as neutral parties between archdevils and demon lords.

trait.name: Telepathic Bond
entries: The succubus ignores the range restriction on its telepathy when communicating with a creature it has charmed. The two don’t even need to be on the same plane of existence.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The succubus makes two Claw attacks or one Claw attack and one Draining Kiss attack. It can replace one attack with Charm.

action.name: Claw (Fiend Form Only)
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) slashing damage plus 7 (2d6) psychic damage.

action.name: Draining Kiss
entries: Melee Spell Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one willing creature, or a creature that is charmed, incapacitated, or restrained. Hit: 15 (3d6 + 5) psychic damage, and the target’s HP maximum is reduced by an amount equal to the damage taken. This reduction lasts until the target finishes a long rest. The target dies if this effect reduces its HP maximum to 0.

action.name: Charm
entries: One Humanoid the succubus can see within 30 feet of it must succeed on a DC 15 WIS save or be magically charmed for 1 day, until the succubus dies, or until the succubus takes a bonus action to end the effect. The charmed target obeys the succubus’s verbal or telepathic commands. If the target suffers any harm or receives a suicidal command, it can repeat the save, ending the effect on itself on a success. If the target successfully saves against the effect, or if the effect on it ends, the target is immune to this succubus’s Charm for the next 24 hours. The succubus can have only one target charmed at a time. If it charms another, the effect on the previous target ends.

bonus.name: Change Shape
entries: The succubus transforms into a Small or Medium Humanoid or back into its true form, which is Fiend. Without wings, it loses its flying speed. Its statistics, other than its size, are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn’t transformed. It reverts to its true form if it dies.

bonus.name: Etherealness
entries: The succubus magically enters the Ethereal Plane from the Material Plane, or vice versa.

reaction.name: Text
entries: Defend Me, Love! When a creature the succubus can see within 5 feet of a creature charmed by the succubus attacks the succubus, the succubus can command the charmed creature to use its reaction to make one melee weapon attack against the attacker. Tarrasque A tremendous reptilian beast thunders across the landscape, its crashing steps and thrashing spiked tail leaving destruction in its wake.

reaction.name: Massive Predator
entries: The mighty tarrasque is a massive, six-legged, reptilian predator that towers over even the tallest buildings and whose thunderous steps shake mountains. Its body is covered in scales and armored plates with spines and spikes running the length of its shell-like back. Its jaws are filled with razor-sharp fangs that can splinter trees and swallow whole herds of deer in one swallow, and its forelimbs end in huge claws that dig out swaths of land when it burrows.

reaction.name: Ancient Hunger Manifest
entries: The tarrasque is the manifestation of the last breath of a dying god of hunger. The tarrasque is all that remains of that deity, and many of the cults that once worshiped it now worship the tarrasque, unaware there was ever a god before the tarrasque. As the embodiment of that deity, the tarrasque is filled with near- endless hunger, but it still has a corporeal form that can consume only so much at any one time.

reaction.name: Slumbering Terror
entries: Because its corporeal form takes ages to digest its massive meals, the tarrasque spends most of its life in hibernation deep in the earth for centuries at a time. When it awakens, it surfaces for a few days or weeks and carves a swathe of destruction across the land, devouring everything organic it can catch and obliterating anything in its way. Its infrequent appearance leads many to believe there is only one, timeless tarrasque, while others believe the tarrasque is purely a myth to frighten the gullible—until it comes calling at their doorstep. A TARRASQUE’S LAIR A tarrasque’s lair is typically a cave or cavern it has dug into the side of a mountain or deep beneath the surface of the earth. Imbued over millennia with the tarrasque’s otherworldly hunger, the lair reacts to intruders as hungrily as its master. A tarrasque’s lair is often littered with the undigestible wood, metal, and stone that it coughs up during its centuries-long slumber. LAIR ACTIONS On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the tarrasque can take a lair action to cause one of the following effects; the tarrasque can’t use the same effect two rounds in a row:
• Endless Hunger. The tarrasque’s nigh-endless hunger

spreads to up to three creatures it can see within 120 feet of it. Each target must succeed on a DC 27 CON save or be overcome with hunger until initiative count 20 on the next round. A creature overcome with hunger can’t use reactions, and on its turn, it can use either an action or a bonus action, not both. Regardless of the creature’s abilities or magic items, a creature overcome with hunger can’t make more than one melee or ranged attack during its turn.
• Growling Stomach. A rumble shakes the ground and

an earthen maw opens on a point the tarrasque can see within 120 feet of it. Each creature on the ground within 30 feet of that point must succeed on a DC 27 DEX save or be knocked prone. Each creature that failed the save while within 10 feet of that point is also restrained as it becomes half-buried by the earth. A creature, including the restrained creature, can take an action to free the restrained creature by succeeding on a DC 27 STR check.
• Overfull. The bloated sensation of an overfull belly

overwhelms up to three creatures the tarrasque can see within 120 feet of it. Each target must succeed on a DC 27 CON save or feel overfull until initiative count 20 on the next round. A creature that feels overfull takes a −2 penalty to AC and DEX checks and DEX saves, and its speed is halved. REGIONAL EFFECTS The region containing a tarrasque’s lair is warped by its hungering presence, which creates one or more of the following effects:
• Food Aplenty. Flora and fauna, especially prey animals
and crops, within 10 miles of the lair are more abundant, producing up to half again as much as normal animals and plants of their type.
• Sated and Sleepy. Creatures near the lair become more
complacent and sleepier after eating. A creature that eats a meal within 5 miles of the lair is vulnerable to the charmed and unconscious conditions for up to 1 hour after eating.
• Seeping Hunger. The tarrasque’s hunger seeps into the

countryside around its lair. Prepared food within 1 mile of the lair spoils faster, some of it magically consumed by the slumbering tarrasque. If the tarrasque dies, these effects fade in 1d10 days, except for Food Aplenty, which fades within 1 year.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the tarrasque fails a save, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The tarrasque has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Monstrosity Resilience
entries: The tarrasque is resistant to exhaustion.

trait.name: Reflective Hide
entries: Any time the tarrasque is targeted by a magic missile spell, a line spell, or a spell that requires a ranged attack roll, roll a d6. On a 1 to 5, the tarrasque is unaffected. On a 6, the tarrasque is unaffected, and the effect is reflected back at the caster as though it originated from the tarrasque, turning the caster into the target.

trait.name: Siege Behemoth
entries: The tarrasque deals triple damage to objects and structures.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The tarrasque uses its Frightful Presence. It then makes one Bite attack, four Claw attacks, and one Spiked Tail attack, or it makes five Acid Spit attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +19 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 36 (4d12 + 10) piercing damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a DC 27 DEX save or be swallowed by the tarrasque. A swallowed creature is blinded and restrained, it has total cover against attacks and other effect outside of the tarrasque, and it takes 42 (12d6) acid damage at the start of each of the tarrasque’s turns. If the tarrasque takes 60 damage or more on a single turn from a creature inside it, the tarrasque must succeed on a DC 20 CON save at the end of that turn or regurgitate all swallowed creatures, which fall prone in a space within 10 feet of the tarrasque. If the tarrasque dies, a swallowed creature is no longer restrained by it and can escape from the corpse using 20 feet of movement, exiting prone.

action.name: Claw
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +19 to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. Hit: 28 (4d8 + 10) slashing damage.

action.name: Spiked Tail
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +19 to hit, reach 20 ft., one target. Hit: 20 (3d6 + 10) bludgeoning damage and 13 (3d8) piercing damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a DC 27 STR save or be knocked prone.

action.name: Acid Spit
entries: Ranged Weapon Attack: +12 to hit, range 100/400 ft., one target. Hit: 36 (6d10 + 3) acid damage.

action.name: Frightful Presence
entries: Each creature of the tarrasque’s choice within 120 feet of it and aware of it must succeed on a DC 27 WIS save or be frightened for 1 minute. A creature can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. If a creature’s save is successful or the effect ends for it, the creature is immune to the tarrasque’s Frightful Presence for the next 24 hours.

legendary.name: Text
entries: The tarrasque can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action option can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature’s turn. The tarrasque regains spent legendary actions at the start of its turn.

legendary.name: Detect
entries: The tarrasque automatically detects every creature and trap within 60 feet of it, pinpointing the current location of each.

legendary.name: Move
entries: The tarrasque moves up to its speed without provoking opportunity attacks.

legendary.name: Tail Attack
entries: The tarrasque makes one Tail attack.

legendary.name: Tail Swipe (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The tarrasque whips its spiked tail in a wide arc, flailing it in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a DC 27 DEX save. On a failure, a creature takes 45 (10d8) piercing damage and is knocked prone. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn’t knocked prone. Treant A massive tree stands on two trunk-like legs and feet of twisting roots. A gnarled humanoid face protrudes from the bark beneath its canopy, and two massive branch-like arms reach out from its sides.

legendary.name: Sentient Trees
entries: Denizens of ancient forests, treants are powerful defenders of the arboreal lands they call home. These sentient plants are often solitary folk. A treant acts as steward of its woodland home, wandering and ensuring that the forest remains healthy and thriving. Greater ranges of woodland may have a pair of treants as wardens. Even larger tracts, or especially ancient or magical woodlands, can have three or more treant guardians, who refer to themselves collectively as the Grove.

legendary.name: Wise Defenders
entries: T reants are sedentary creatures, prone to long periods of immobility. Wise and powerful, they often withdraw into their minds for intensive contemplation and meditation. When their homelands are in direct danger, however, treants are swift to both anger and action. They embody the power of nature itself and can easily reduce buildings and defenses to rubble, allowing nature to retake the land.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the treant remains motionless, it is indistinguishable from a normal tree.

trait.name: Plant Resilience
entries: The treant is resistant to exhaustion and to the paralyzed, petrified, and unconscious conditions.

trait.name: Siege Monster
entries: The treant deals double damage to objects and structures.

trait.name: Speak with Plants
entries: The treant can communicate with Plants and nonmagical plants as if they shared a language.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The treant makes three Slam attacks, or it makes two Throw Rock attacks. If two Slam attacks hit one creature, the target must succeed on a DC 17 STR save or be knocked prone.

action.name: Slam
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 22 (4d6 + 6) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Throw Rock
entries: Ranged Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, range 60/180 ft., one target. Hit: 33 (5d10 + 6) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Animate Trees (1/Day)
entries: The treant magically animates 2 trees or 2d6 shrubs it can see within 60 feet of it. The trees use the statistics of an awakened tree, and the shrubs use the statistics of an awakened shrub (see Animals Appendix). The animated plants act as allies of the treant, obeying its spoken commands. The plants remain for 1 hour, until the treant dies, or until the treant dismisses them as a bonus action.

bonus.name: Halt Intruders
entries: The treant calls to the roots beneath up to two creatures it can see within 30 feet of it. Each target must succeed on a DC 17 STR save or be restrained by roots until the end of its next turn. The treant can target only creatures in contact with the ground and can’t target creatures in contact with a manufactured floor, such as the wooden floor of a building. 3 11 TT Troll This hunched giant stares hungrily with wide eyes. Baring massive fangs, it reaches out with long, hooked claws.

bonus.name: Variable Appearance
entries: T roll appearance varies widely, and they are prone to a variety of cosmetic mutations. Skin tones and body types include scaled or slimy, broad and rangy, and even somewhat twiggy or stonelike in some cases. Some sport shaggy mops of hair and even beards, while others are completely hairless.

bonus.name: Bullies
entries: T rolls are natural bullies, killing to sate their appetites. They enjoy extorting food and wealth from weaker creatures through threats of violence.

bonus.name: Regeneration
entries: The troll’s most notable feature is its powerful regenerative capabilities. Given time, a troll can recover from nearly any wound, even complete dismemberment or beheading. Only wounds delivered with acid or fire can overcome a troll’s regeneration.

trait.name: Giant Attributes
entries: The troll is resistant to the stunned condition, and it is vulnerable to the prone condition.

trait.name: Heightened Smell
entries: The troll’s Perception is 17 while perceiving by smell.

trait.name: Regeneration
entries: The troll regains 10 HP at the start of its turn. If the troll takes acid or fire damage, this trait doesn’t function at the start of the troll’s next turn. The troll dies only if it starts its turn with 0 HP and doesn’t regenerate.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The troll makes one Bite attack and two Claw attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (2d8 + 4) piercing damage.

action.name: Claw
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) piercing damage.

bonus.name: Ravenous Frenzy (52 HP or Fewer)
entries: Desperate for a meal as its injuries mount, the troll moves up to half its speed and makes one Bite attack against a creature it can see within range. The troll then regains HP equal to half the damage dealt. VARIANT: STONE SKIN TROLL Some trolls are turned to stone by sunlight or by spells that create sunlight. Such a troll’s Regeneration trait is suppressed when it takes radiant damage, not fire damage. In addition, if a stone skin troll takes 20 or more radiant damage on a turn, it must succeed on a DC 15 CON save or be petrified. Unicorn The white coat of this noble, equine form shimmers like moonlight on water. A pearlescent, spiraling horn rises from its head above glowing eyes.

bonus.name: Solitary
entries: Unicorns are solitary creatures. They shun contact with most other creatures and even avoid more than casual contact with the animals of their domain. Predators instinctively avoid unicorns, giving them a wide berth when hunting. Unicorns can tolerate the presence of fey, druids, and clerics devoted to nature and the preservation of the forest, though a unicorn’s full trust often takes years to earn. Cleansing Protectors. Stalwart opponents of corruption, unicorns allow no taint of unnatural forces to encroach on their home, nor do they allow any lasting damage to be done by ignorant trespassers. The unicorn typically ignores creatures that come into its realm to hunt, gather herbs and fruits, or on similar missions without malice. Those without corruption or true malice in their hearts who unknowingly despoil or deforest the unicorn’s domain are swiftly turned away and urged never to return. Creatures of corruption, undeath, and similar forces that pervert or destroy the natural world, however, are met with the unicorn’s full might and wrath.

bonus.name: Mythical Magic
entries: Many legends surround unicorns, especially with regards to the restorative magic within their horns. Many seek out unicorns, hoping to have an illness cured or a fatal injury reversed, though only supplicants with the purest of hearts ever seem to find the elusive unicorn. Those seeking the unicorn’s power without pure intent risk a fatal encounter with a unicorn. A UNICORN’S LAIR A unicorn’s lair is a place of serene, solitary beauty: a fern-filled grotto, an oaken bower dripping with mistletoe, a sunlit glade alive with wildflowers, a clearing where a waterfall plummets into a clear pool, and similar. When encountered in its lair, the unicorn has a challenge rating of 6 (2,300 XP). LAIR ACTIONS On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the unicorn can take a lair action to cause one of the following effects; the unicorn can’t use the same effect two rounds in a row:
• Grasping Vines. Vines reach out from the ground and

trees in the lair toward up to two creatures the unicorn can see within 60 feet of it. Each target must succeed on a DC 14 DEX save or take 5 (2d4) piercing damage and be restrained. While restrained, a creature takes 5 (2d4) piercing damage at the end of each of its turns. A creature, including the restrained creature, can take its action to free the restrained creature by succeeding on a DC 14 STR check.
• Light Beam. A beam of sunlight or moonlight,

whichever is appropriate for the time of day, shines down in a 10-foot-radius, 20-foot-high cylinder centered on a point the unicorn can see within 120 feet of it. A creature that enters the beam’s area for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there must succeed on a DC 14 CON save or take 7 (2d6) radiant damage. The unicorn can choose if this beam harms Beasts and Plants in its area. The beam lasts until the unicorn uses this lair action again or until the unicorn dies.
• Shimmering Coat. All the light in the lair bends

toward the unicorn, reflecting off its brilliant coat. Each hostile creature within 15 feet of the unicorn and that can see it must make a DC 14 DEX save. On a failure, a creature takes 14 (4d6) radiant damage and is blinded until the end of its next turn. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn’t blinded. REGIONAL EFFECTS The region containing a unicorn’s lair is altered by its protective presence, which creates one or more of the following effects.
• Abundant Flora. Plant growth is more abundant
within 1 mile of the unicorn’s lair, making the area difficult terrain for all except the unicorn.
• Hinder Corruption. Aberrations and Undead within 3

miles of the lair are hindered while moving through the area. Roots rise to trip them, vines hang in their path, predators harry them, and similar small occurrences happen regularly, disrupting the creatures’ movement deeper into the forest.
• Vibrant Fauna and Flora. The animals and plants

within 10 miles of the lair are healthier, carry and suffer fewer diseases, and provide up to 50 percent more nourishment than standard animals and plants of their sizes and types to creatures that consume them.

trait.name: Celestial Resilience
entries: The unicorn is resistant to radiant damage.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The unicorn has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Magic Weapons
entries: The unicorn’s weapon attacks are magical.

trait.name: Sense the Unnatural
entries: The unicorn can pinpoint the location of Aberrations and Undead within 60 feet of it.

trait.name: Speak with Beasts and Plants
entries: The unicorn can communicate with Beasts and Plants as if they shared a language.

trait.name: Woodland Walk
entries: Difficult terrain composed of nonmagical plants doesn’t cost the unicorn extra movement. In addition, the unicorn can pass through nonmagical plants without being slowed by them and without taking damage from them if they have thorns, spines, or a similar hazard.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The unicorn makes one Hooves attack and one Horn attack, or it makes two Horn Bolt attacks.

action.name: Hooves
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Horn
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (1d8 + 4) piercing damage plus 10 (3d6) radiant damage.

action.name: Horn Bolt
entries: Ranged Spell Attack: +6 to hit, range 60 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (3d6 + 3) radiant damage.

action.name: Unicorn Magic
entries: The unicorn can cast the dancing lights and druidcraft cantrips at will, requiring no material components and using WIS as the spellcasting ability.

action.name: Healing Touch (3/Day)
entries: The unicorn touches another creature with its horn. The target magically regains 11 (2d8 + 2) HP and is freed from all curses, diseases, and poisons afflicting it.

reaction.name: Shimmering Shield
entries: When a creature the unicorn can see within 30 feet of it, including the unicorn, is attacked, the unicorn can surround it with a shimmering shield of radiant light. The target adds 3 to its AC against the attack.

legendary.name: Text
entries: The unicorn can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action option can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature’s turn. The unicorn regains spent legendary actions at the start of its turn.

legendary.name: Canter
entries: The unicorn moves up to half is speed without provoking opportunity attacks.

legendary.name: Radiant Stomp (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The unicorn raises its hooves glowing with golden light and brings them down with a reverberating stomp. Each hostile creature within 10 feet of the unicorn must make a DC 14 STR save. On a failure, a creature takes 10 (3d6) radiant damage and is knocked prone. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn’t knocked prone.

legendary.name: Heal Self (Costs 3 Actions)
entries: The unicorn magically regains 11 (2d8 + 2) HP . V ampires The humanoid in regal clothing speaks in a soft, soothing voice. A smile splits its pale face, revealing glistening fangs.

legendary.name: Legendary Undead
entries: One of the most powerful, storied, and feared undead, vampires are renowned for their thirst for blood. Unlike most other undead, a vampire looks as it did in life, though often with a paler complexion. This allows them to walk mostly unhindered among their preferred prey, living humanoids. They have subtle indicators that reveal their true natures to astute observers. A vampire casts neither shadow nor reflection. They are more feral and prone to outbursts of primal emotions—lust, fear, anger—especially if provoked by a canny individual during social interactions.

legendary.name: Powerful Emotions
entries: Vampires tend to be more primal and emotional than their humanoid counterparts. They can become easily fixated on creatures, people, or things that arouse their emotions. An interest or hobby from life can turn into a disturbing obsession in undeath, leading the vampire to commit atrocities to obtain a rare tome for its prized library, to possess the love of its former life, or even to extinguish a once-friendly rival.

legendary.name: Blood Drinker
entries: A vampire feeds upon the blood of the living. When a vampire completely drains a humanoid of blood then feeds that corpse a single drop of its own blood, it creates a lesser vampire bound to its service, known as a vampire spawn. If a vampire dies, any spawn it created become free-willed and can eventually ascend to become full vampires.

legendary.name: Blood Thralls
entries: Vampires need a significant amount of blood to sustain themselves, but killing several humanoids in a small area can prove troublesome for any cover the vampire is attempting to maintain. Because of this, many vampires collect a cadre of willing humanoids who provide the vampire with small amounts of blood each day. T o ensure loyalty, the vampire regularly gives some of its blood to these humanoids, turning them into vampire thralls. Vampire thralls are not themselves undead, though many are hopeful they soon will be. They fawn over their vampire master, performing whatever actions the vampire requests and believing each act of obedience leads them one step closer to their dreams of becoming vampires. A VAMPIRE’S LAIR Creatures of the night, vampires must rest in a coffin, crypt, or other burial site during the day. Vulnerable during this time, vampires select lairs where their resting places are defended by stout walls, deadly traps, or stalwart guardians. An abandoned fortress, hidden catacomb, or fortified tower or a location humanoids consider cursed, forbidden, or haunted all serve well as vampire lairs. LAIR ACTIONS On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the vampire can take a lair action to cause one of the following effects; the vampire can’t use the same effect two rounds in a row:
• Eclipse. Each source of nonmagical light within 30

feet of a point the vampire can see within 60 feet of it is extinguished. Each source of magical light within 30 feet of that point is diminished: bright light becomes dim light, and dim light becomes darkness. If the light is sunlight, it is diminished to bright light instead. This diminishing effect lasts until initiative count 20 on the next round.
• Sanguine Tithe. Blood rains in a 30-foot radius,

60-foot-high cylinder centered on a point the vampire can see within 120 feet of it. Each hostile creature that enters the area for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there must succeed on a DC 17 DEX save or be coated in magical blood until initiative count 20 of the next round. Each time the vampire deals damage to a creature coated in magical blood, the vampire regains 5 (2d4) HP . The rain lasts for 1 minute, until the vampire uses this lair action, or until the vampire dies.
• Shrieking Storm. A 30-foot radius cloud of shrieking

bats briefly appears centered on a point the vampire can see within 120 feet of it. Each creature in the cloud must succeed on a DC 17 DEX save or be deafened until initiative count 20 on the next round. If a creature in the cloud is concentrating on a spell, the creature must succeed on a DC 17 CON save or lose concentration on the spell. REGIONAL EFFECTS The region containing a vampire’s lair is changed by its deathly presence, which creates one or more of the following effects:
• Grave Visions. Within 5 miles of the lair, an illusion of

the vampire occasionally hovers above graves, corpses, or piles of bones. The vampire knows the direction and distance to any creature that speaks to, touches, attacks, or otherwise interacts with such an illusion. Once touched or attacked, the illusion shatters into a swarm of bats or rats that remains for 1d4 rounds before scattering.
• Healthy Stock. A Humanoid that spends at least 1
year within 5 miles of the lair is resistant to diseases and poisons while within 5 miles of the lair. This effect ends if the Humanoid spends more than 7 days outside this area.
• Predator’s Temper. Within 10 miles of the lair,

bats, rats, and wolves reflect the vampire’s current mood. They are more aggressive when the vampire is angry, friendlier when the vampire is happy, vocal with excessive howls and shrieks when the vampire is sullen, and similar. If the vampire dies, these effects fade in 1d10 days.

monster.name: Vampire

other: climb 30 ft. (40 ft. in wolf form

languages: the languages it knew in life

trait.name: Hungry Dead Nature
entries: The vampire doesn’t require air or sleep. In addition, it must consume at least 80 ounces of blood (approximately half the blood in a Medium Humanoid’s body) every 24 hours, or it loses its immunity to exhaustion and risks starvation until it does so. While it has any levels of exhaustion from starvation, the vampire can’t remove levels of exhaustion until it consumes at least 160 ounces of blood.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the vampire fails a save, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Misty Escape
entries: When it drops to 0 HP outside its resting place, the vampire transforms into a cloud of mist (as in the Mist Form bonus action) instead of falling unconscious, provided it isn’t in sunlight or running water. If it can’t transform, it is destroyed. While it has 0 HP in mist form, it can’t revert to its vampire form, and it must reach its resting place within 2 hours or be destroyed. Once in its resting place, it reverts to its vampire form. It is then paralyzed until it regains at least 1 HP . After spending 1 hour in its resting place with 0 HP , it regains 1 HP .

trait.name: Regeneration
entries: The vampire regains 20 HP at the start of its turn if it has at least 1 HP and isn’t in sunlight or running water. If the vampire takes radiant damage or damage from holy water, this trait doesn’t function at the start of the vampire’s next turn.

trait.name: Resting Place
entries: The vampire has a coffin, grave, sarcophagus, or similar location just large enough for it to lie down designated as its resting place. While in its resting place, the vampire doesn’t suffer exhaustion levels from starvation and has keensense out to a range of 120 feet.

trait.name: Spider Climb
entries: The vampire can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

trait.name: Vampire Weaknesses
entries: The vampire has the following flaws:

trait.name: Forbiddance
entries: The vampire can’t enter a residence without an invitation from one of the occupants.

trait.name: Harmed by Running Water
entries: The vampire takes 20 acid damage if it ends its turn in running water.

trait.name: Stake to the Heart
entries: If a piercing weapon made of wood is driven into the vampire’s heart while the vampire is incapacitated in its resting place, the vampire is paralyzed until the stake is removed.

trait.name: Sunlight Hypersensitivity
entries: The vampire takes 20 radiant damage when it starts its turn in sunlight. While in sunlight, it has disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks.

trait.name: Vampiric Resilience
entries: The vampire is immune to exhaustion.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The vampire can use Charm. It then makes three Bite or Claw attacks. It can replace one attack with a Draining Bite attack. If two Claw attacks hit one Large or smaller creature, the target is grappled (escape DC 17).

action.name: Bite (Bat, Rat, or Wolf Form Only)
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) piercing damage plus 14 (4d6) necrotic damage.

action.name: Claw (Vampire Form Only)
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) slashing damage plus 14 (4d6) necrotic damage.

action.name: Draining Bite (Bat or Vampire Form Only)
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 5 ft., one willing creature, or a creature that is grappled by the vampire, incapacitated, or restrained. Hit: 9 (2d4 + 4) piercing damage plus 14 (4d6) necrotic damage. The target’s HP maximum is reduced by an amount equal to the necrotic damage taken, and the vampire regains HP equal to that amount. The reduction lasts until the target finishes a long rest. The target dies if this effect reduces its HP maximum to 0. A Humanoid slain in this way and then fed at least one drop of the vampire’s blood rises the following night as a vampire spawn under the vampire’s control.

action.name: Charm (Vampire Form Only)
entries: One Humanoid the vampire can see within 30 feet of it must succeed on a DC 17 WIS save or be magically charmed for 1 day, until the vampire dies, or until the vampire takes a bonus action to end the effect. The charmed target obeys the vampire’s verbal commands and is a willing target for the vampire’s Draining Bite attack. If the target suffers any harm or receives a suicidal command, it can repeat the save, ending the effect on itself on a success. The vampire can have up to two creatures charmed in this way at a time. If it charms a third, the effect on the first target ends.

action.name: Children of the Night
entries: The vampire magically calls 2d4 swarms of bats or rats, provided that the sun isn’t up. While outdoors, the vampire can call 3d6 wolves instead. The called creatures arrive in 1d4 rounds, acting as allies of the vampire and obeying its spoken commands. The Beasts remain for 1 hour, until the vampire dies, or until the vampire dismisses them as a bonus action. The vampire can have any number of such Beasts under its control at one time, provided the combined total CR of the Beasts is no higher than 4.

bonus.name: Change Shape
entries: The vampire transforms into a Tiny bat, Tiny rat, Medium wolf, or back into its true form, which is Undead. Its statistics, other than its size and speed, are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing transforms with it, but anything it is carrying doesn’t transform with it. It reverts to its true form if it dies.

bonus.name: Mist Form
entries: The vampire transforms into a Medium cloud of mist or back into its true form, which is Undead. While in mist form, it can’t take any actions, speak, or manipulate objects. It is weightless, has a flying speed of 20 feet, can hover, and can enter a hostile creature’s space and stop there. In addition, if air can pass through a space, the mist can do so without squeezing, and it can’t pass through water. It has advantage on STR, DEX, and CON saves, and it is immune to all nonmagical damage, except the damage it takes from sunlight.

legendary.name: Text
entries: The vampire can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action option can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature’s turn. The vampire regains spent legendary actions at the start of its turn.

legendary.name: Move
entries: The vampire moves up to its speed without provoking opportunity attacks.

legendary.name: Call Children (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The vampire uses Children of the Night.

legendary.name: Excessive Bleeding (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: Each creature within 15 feet of the vampire that doesn’t have all its HP must succeed on a DC 17 CON save or lose 14 (4d6) HP as its wounds bleed profusely. Before the end of its next turn, the vampire has advantage on the next attack roll it makes against a creature that failed this save.

monster.name: Vampire Spawn
languages: the languages it knew in life

trait.name: Hungry Dead Nature
entries: As the vampire.

trait.name: Regeneration
entries: The vampire spawn regains 10 HP at the start of its turn if it has at least 1 HP and isn’t in sunlight or running water. If the vampire takes radiant damage or damage from holy water, this trait doesn’t function at the start of the vampire’s next turn.

trait.name: Resting Place
entries: As the vampire.

trait.name: Spider Climb
entries: As the vampire Vampire Weaknesses. As the vampire.

trait.name: Vampiric Resilience
entries: The vampire is immune to exhaustion.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The vampire spawn makes two Claw attacks. It can replace one attack with a Draining Bite attack. If both Claw attacks hit one Large or smaller creature, the target is grappled (escape DC 14).

action.name: Claw
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. Hit: 8 (2d4 + 3) slashing damage plus 10 (3d6) necrotic damage.

action.name: Draining Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one willing creature, or a creature that is grappled by the vampire, incapacitated, or restrained. Hit: 8 (2d4 + 3) piercing damage plus 10 (3d6) necrotic damage. The target’s HP maximum is reduced by an amount equal to the necrotic damage taken, and the vampire regains HP equal to that amount. The reduction lasts until the target finishes a long rest. The target dies if this effect reduces its HP maximum to 0.

bonus.name: Call Blood
entries: The vampire calls to the blood within one injured creature it can see within 10 feet of it, causing blood to pour from the creature’s wounds. If the target doesn’t have all its HP , it must make a DC 14 CON save, losing 7 (2d6) HP on a failed save, or losing half as much HP on a successful one. Before the end of its next turn, the vampire has advantage on the next attack roll it makes against a creature that failed the save.

trait.name: Blood-Bound
entries: If the thrall has not consumed 1 ounce of blood from a vampire within the past week, it is poisoned for 1 week, and it loses darkvision and its Limited Regeneration, Spider Climb, and Sunlight Sensitivity traits until it consumes 1 ounce of vampire blood.

trait.name: Limited Regeneration
entries: The thrall regains 2 HP at the start of its turn if it has at least 1 HP and isn’t in sunlight. If the thrall takes radiant damage, this trait doesn’t function at the start of the thrall’s next turn.

trait.name: Spider Climb
entries: The thrall can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

trait.name: Sunlight Sensitivity
entries: While in sunlight, the thrall has disadvantage on attack rolls, and its Perception is 8 when perceiving by sight.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The vampire thrall makes two Rapier attacks.

action.name: Rapier
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) piercing damage.

reaction.name: Repay the Gift
entries: When a friendly vampire the thrall can see within 5 feet of it is hit by an attack that would reduce the vampire to 0 HP , the thrall can give some of its life force to save the vampire. The thrall loses 10 (3d6) HP , and the vampire regains HP equal to that amount. Voidling Writhing black tendrils stretch from this indistinct orb of pure shadow. The faintest flicker of something green appears for a moment in the center of the globe.

reaction.name: Called from Darkness
entries: Voidlings are creatures of the Void that are drawn to mortal realms by foul, corrupt magic. They are typically summoned by those hungry for power at any cost, and—despite their dark reputation— they serve well for years or even decades, until one day they turn on their summoners. If they slay their summoner, they grow in strength and return to the Void, in this way growing from lesser to fully fledged voidlings. Exactly what voidlings seek when they have not been summoned—and what triggers their betrayals—is a mystery.

reaction.name: Cold Tendrils
entries: Creatures of utter darkness, voidlings consist of lashing tendrils of shadow. The tendrils meet at a central sphere in which something like an eye appears intermittently.

reaction.name: Sustained by Darkness
entries: Voidlings are said to devour life and knowledge, and they subsist on darkness. The places they inhabit are known for their dank chill and obscurity.

trait.name: Aberrant Resilience
entries: The voidling is resistant to the charmed, frightened, paralyzed, and stunned conditions, and it has advantage on saves against spells or effects that would alter its form.

trait.name: Darkness Regeneration
entries: While in an area of dim light or darkness, the voidling regains 2 HP at the start of its turn if it has at least 1 HP .

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The voidling has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Void Dweller
entries: The voidling doesn’t require air, food, drink, sleep, or ambient pressure, and it is immune to the effects of Void terrain. In addition, it can fly through the Void as if through air.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The lesser voidling makes three Shadow Tendril or Necrotic Bolt attacks.

action.name: Shadow Tendril
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d4 + 3) bludgeoning damage plus 7 (2d6) necrotic damage.

action.name: Necrotic Bolt
entries: Ranged Spell Attack: +5 to hit, range 120 ft., one target. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) necrotic damage.

action.name: Necrotic Burst (Recharge 5–6)
entries: The voidling releases a burst of necrotic energy. Each creature within 10 feet of it must make a DC 13 WIS save, taking 21 (6d6) necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

trait.name: Aberrant Resilience
entries: The voidling is resistant to the charmed, frightened, paralyzed, and stunned conditions, and it has advantage on saves against spells or effects that would alter its form.

trait.name: Darkness Regeneration
entries: While in an area of dim light or darkness, the voidling regains 5 HP at the start of its turn if it has at least 1 HP .

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The voidling has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects..

trait.name: Void Dweller
entries: The voidling doesn’t require air, food, drink, sleep, or ambient pressure, and it is immune to the effects of Void terrain. In addition, it can fly through the Void as if through air.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The voidling makes four Shadow Tendril or Necrotic Bolt attacks. If two Necrotic Bolt attacks hit one creature, the target must succeed on a DC 17 WIS save or be frightened until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Shadow Tendril
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (1d6 + 5) bludgeoning damage plus 10 (3d6) necrotic damage.

action.name: Necrotic Bolt
entries: Ranged Spell Attack: +7 to hit, range 120 ft., one target. Hit: 17 (4d6 + 3) necrotic damage. Shadow’s Grasp (Recharge 5–6). VOID-POWERED SPELLCASTERS Most voidlings that leave the Void do so because they were summoned by spellcasters interested in dabbling in the magic of the Void. While a voidling’s time in service is never definite, its presence can empower its summoner, if it is summoned correctly. Voidlings summoned and bound in this special ritual have the following trait:

action.name: Void-Powered Aura
entries: When a friendly creature within 10 feet of the voidling casts a spell that deals necrotic damage, summons or binds a creature, or that causes the charmed or frightened condition, the spellcaster has a +1 bonus to the spell’s attack rolls, and the save DC for the spell increases by 1. If the voidling is below half its HP maximum, these benefits each increase by 1 as the voidling’s fading power seeps into the area around it. The voidling releases a wave of small, grasping tendrils of shadow. Each creature within 20 feet of it must make a DC 17 DEX save. On a failure, a creature takes 49 (14d6) necrotic damage and is restrained by tendrils of shadow for 1 minute. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn’t restrained. A creature can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. Wickerbeast A human-like figure made of woven branches squats in the underbrush. A small mirror between its bramble horns flashes briefly with a glint of light.

action.name: Wooden Fey
entries: Enigmatic fey, wickerbeasts are a rare but chilling sight. Living in remote regions, these creatures subsist on the souls of other beings. They take the shapes of various humanoids or animals completely made of woven wooden branches, but regardless of their form, each carries an ornate, golden mirror embedded in its body.

action.name: Cursed Origins
entries: The true origins of the wickerbeast are unknown, but fey scholars generally agree that these creatures suffer from some form of curse. The wickerbeasts endlessly search for souls to possess, navigating life through what they can perceive in their mirrors. However, since these creatures are incapable of speech, their exact motivations remain a mystery.

action.name: Captured Souls
entries: The inside of a wickerbeast’s woven body is hollow, and the souls they capture float inside this space. Wickerbeasts do not have defined facial features, but when they possess a captured soul, light shines through the branches of their faces, creating an imitation of eyes. Many believe that a creature is safe from a passing wickerbeast if they can see its two eyes glowing.

monster.name: Wickerbeast
resistNoteFromTraits: bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from

trait.name: Creeping Growth
entries: The wickerbeast’s presence causes nearby creeping vines and roots to rapidly grow along the ground around it. The area within 15 feet of the wickerbeast is difficult terrain. This difficult terrain doesn’t cost the wickerbeast extra movement.

trait.name: Fey Resilience
entries: The wickerbeast is resistant to the charmed and unconscious conditions.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The wickerbeast has advantage on saves against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The wickerbeast makes two Soul-Rending Slam attacks. If both attacks hit one creature, the target must succeed on a DC 15 CHA save or have its soul partially rent from its body for 1 minute. While the creature’s soul is rent in this way, it has disadvantage on saves against the wickerbeast’s Capture Souls.

action.name: Soul-Rending Slam
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (2d8 + 4) bludgeoning damage plus 10 (3d6) psychic damage.

action.name: Capture Souls (Recharge 5–6)
entries: The wickerbeast absorbs the souls of nearby creatures. Each creature within 15 feet of it that isn’t a Celestial, Construct, or Fiend must make a DC 15 CON save. On a failure, a creature takes 28 (8d6) psychic damage, and its soul is stolen by the wickerbeast. On a success, a creature takes half the damage, and its soul isn’t stolen. A creature with a stolen soul can’t regain HP , and if it dies while its soul is stolen, it can’t be restored to life. Stolen souls are returned to their owners, ending the effect, when the wickerbeast dies or if the wickerbeast wills a stolen soul returned (no action required).

bonus.name: Enchant
entries: The wickerbeast turns its mirror toward one creature it can see within 30 feet of it. If the target can see the wickerbeast’s mirror, the target must succeed on a DC 15 CHA save or have its speed reduced to 0 and be charmed until the end of its next turn, as it witnesses in the mirror some great wrong it has committed, might commit, or that was committed against it. Wight The armored corpse strides with purpose, clutching a bared blade. The withered flesh of its face is twisted with rage, and its eyes are ablaze with ethereal light.

bonus.name: Deathly Warriors
entries: A wight can rise from the corpse of an individual that died a sudden and extremely violent death, or it can rise from the corpse of a cruel and merciless individual who lived a violent life. Regardless of the circumstances, wights naturally arise only from the corpses of soldiers, warriors, or similar martially adept individuals who died violently.

bonus.name: Familiar Countenance
entries: A wight appears much as it did in life, but its flesh is decayed or desiccated in places. Wights are intelligent warriors, retaining many of the memories from their lives, and mindless undead instinctively follow their commands, making them highly desirable as lieutenants for undead armies.

bonus.name: Graveyard Terrors
entries: Wights can drain the essence of the living with a touch. When a wight kills a humanoid by draining all its essence, the humanoid rises as a zombie that instinctively follows the wight’s commands.

monster.name: Wight
languages: the languages it knew in life

trait.name: Martial Adept
entries: A manufactured weapon, such as a sword or bow, deals one extra die of its damage when the wight hits with it (included in the attack).

trait.name: Sunlight Sensitivity
entries: While in sunlight, the wight has disadvantage on attack rolls, and its Perception is 8 when perceiving by sight.

trait.name: Undead Nature
entries: The wight doesn’t require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Undead Resilience
entries: The wight is immune to poison damage, to exhaustion, and to the poisoned condition.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The will-o’-wisp makes two Shock attacks.

action.name: Shock
entries: Melee Spell Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) lightning damage.

action.name: Invisibility
entries: The will-o’-wisp and its light magically become invisible until it attacks or uses its Beckon, or until its concentration ends (as if concentrating on a spell).

bonus.name: Beckon
entries: The will-o’-wisp pulses its lightly gently toward one creature it can see within 30 feet of it. The target must succeed on a DC 13 WIS save or use its reaction to move up to its speed toward the will-o’-wisp by the most direct route. It doesn’t avoid opportunity attacks or damaging terrain when moving.

reaction.name: Consume Life
entries: When a creature the will-o’-wisp can see within 15 feet of it is reduced to 0 HP , the will-o’-wisp can consume some of that creature’s fading life. The will-o’- wisp gains 7 (2d6) temporary HP , and the creature has disadvantage on the next death save it makes before the end of the wisp’s next turn.

monster.name: Will-o’-Wisp
languages: the languages it knew in life

trait.name: Ephemeral
entries: The will-o’-wisp can’t wear or carry anything. In addition, it adds its WIS modifier to its AC (included above).

trait.name: Incorporeal Movement
entries: The will-o’-wisp can move through other creatures and objects as if they were difficult terrain. It takes 5 (1d10) force damage if it ends its turn inside an object.

trait.name: Variable Illumination
entries: The will-o’-wisp sheds bright light in a 5- to 20-foot radius and dim light for an additional number of feet equal to the chosen radius. The will-o’-wisp can alter the radius as a bonus action. Winter Wolf Snow dots the back of this large wolf, blending in with its thick, gray-white fur. Its open mouth reveals sharp, powerful teeth, and a cunning awareness lights its blue eyes.

trait.name: Freezing Breath
entries: Winter wolves are large, aggressive wolves who live in arctic environments. Their fur keeps them warm in the freezing temperatures and provides them camouflage in the icy terrain. Like wolves, they hunt in packs, but unlike wolves, they possess a freezing breath that they use to soften up prey before moving in for the kill.

trait.name: Cunning Hunters
entries: Winter wolves are smarter than normal wolves and can even speak, though their words are punctuated with howls and growls. They are valued for their cunning and strength by a variety of arctic humanoids and giants, particularly frost giants, and they are frequently found as guards and companions of such creatures. When not living among other creatures, winter wolves roam their wintry home with their pack or leading packs of wolves.

trait.name: Legendary Origins
entries: While winter wolves don’t have a formal religion, they believe they are descended from a long-forgotten god of winter and wind, owing their unique breath to that god’s divine blood flowing in their veins. Some winter wolves take this claim literally, name themselves an avatar of some existing wintry deity, and gather scores of faithful around themselves to bring the ultimate winter upon the world.

trait.name: Heightened Hearing and Smell
entries: The winter wolf’s Perception is 20 when perceiving by hearing or smell.

trait.name: Monstrosity Resilience
entries: The winter wolf is resistant to exhaustion and to the frightened condition.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The winter wolf has advantage on attack rolls against a creature if at least one of the winter wolf’s allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn’t incapacitated.

trait.name: Snow Camouflage
entries: The winter wolf’s Stealth is 18 while in snowy terrain.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The winter wolf makes two Bite attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) piercing damage plus 4 (1d8) cold damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a DC 13 STR save or be knocked prone.

action.name: Cold Breath (Recharge 5–6)
entries: The winter wolf exhales a blast of freezing wind in a 15-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a DC 13 DEX save, taking 18 (4d8) cold damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

bonus.name: Freezing Nose
entries: The winter wolf touches its nose to a creature it can see within 5 feet of it. The target must succeed on a DC 13 CON save or one of the following parts of its body (the winter wolf’s choice) is encased in ice for 1 minute. The target can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. If the target takes fire damage, it has advantage on the next save it makes to end the effect.
• Body. The target has disadvantage on CON saves and is
vulnerable to the first instance of cold damage it takes each turn.
• Head. The target can’t breathe or speak.
• Lower Limb. The target’s speed is reduced to 0, and it has
disadvantage on DEX checks and DEX saves.
• Upper Limb. The target has disadvantage on weapon
attack rolls made with that limb. Wood Herald This bipedal, human-shaped tree wears leaves and grasses woven together in imitation clothing, and it wields an axe carved from a single piece of wood.

bonus.name: Forest Spirit
entries: When a forest needs a creature to visit the outside world, it grows a specialized plant. This growth might be a reaction to recent use of fire or axes in the forest, or it could be a scout created by an ancient forest powered by primeval intelligence. This wood herald often seeks to evoke empathy from those it meets on its journey to aid its forest home—though a few are vengeful, especially those born in fire. Some druid circles and ancient treants have learned the secret to fashioning their own wood heralds.

bonus.name: Voice of the Forest
entries: As ambassadors for the woodlands, wood heralds are often the first sentient beings encountered by visitors to the forest. They patrol woodland borders and greet visitors to their homes. Within their forests, the heralds guide and heal lost or imperiled travelers to spread a warm regard for the woods. Wood heralds advocate for the woods and advise against taking timber from ancient groves. Though wood heralds seek to be good neighbors, they ultimately owe fealty to the forests and have no qualms about destroying those who refuse to uphold ancient bargains.

bonus.name: Wooden Weapons
entries: T o protect their forest homes, wood heralds don’t cut down trees. They harvest broken branches, dead trees, and fallen leaves for their clothing and weaponry, and some use flint or stone in clubs or spears. In ancient forests where wood heralds are plentiful, some wooden weapons pass from one herald to another when an ancient herald returns to the forest.

trait.name: Plant Camouflage
entries: The wood herald’s Stealth is 18 while in any terrain with ample, obscuring plant life.

trait.name: Plant Resilience
entries: The wood herald is resistant to exhaustion and to the paralyzed, petrified, and unconscious conditions.

trait.name: Woodland Walk
entries: Difficult terrain composed of nonmagical plants doesn’t cost the wood herald extra movement. In addition, the herald can pass through nonmagical plants without being slowed by them and without taking damage from them if they have thorns, spines, or a similar hazard.

action.name: Slam
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (2d4 + 2) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Wooden Greataxe
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (1d12 + 2) slashing damage.

action.name: Hurl Thorn
entries: Ranged Weapon Attack: +3 to hit, range 30/120 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (2d4 + 1) piercing damage.

reaction.name: Brambly Bark
entries: When a creature the wood herald can see hits it with a melee attack while within 5 feet of it, the herald can extend sharp brambles along its body, dealing 2 (1d4) piercing damage to the attacker. Worg A canine as large as a horse bears down, its oily muzzle caked in blood and saliva. Its thick, heavy shoulders and massive neck sway side to side, and with each step, its long, mangy tail drags along behind it.

reaction.name: Aggressive Hunters
entries: An enormous creature resembling a wolf, a worg is a nightmarish hunter that frequently develops a taste for humanoid flesh. Worgs willingly stalk inhospitable terrain in their endless hunt for their next meal, roaming snow fields, volcanic barrens, even stony badlands. Known to collect grisly bounties and to coat their bodies with fetid remains, worgs are more ferocious than any natural beast.

reaction.name: Warren Dweller
entries: Worgs often shelter in warrens, tunnels, and caves in which they can travel quickly to prey and away from predators. Such places are fetid, vile places full of half-eaten corpses that the worg seems to have gorged itself on without consequence.

reaction.name: Goblin Bond
entries: While many beasts of burden must be trained to hold a rider, worgs, who possess an innate, instinctive bond with goblins, tend to willingly carry goblins, and they frequently use the smaller creatures to help them find more bountiful prey. Possessing surprising intelligence, worgs typically speak Goblin as well as their own guttural language. On rare occasions, worgs have even learned to speak and understand Common or Elvish.

trait.name: Heightened Hearing and Smell
entries: The worg’s Perception is 19 while perceiving by hearing or smell.

trait.name: Monstrosity Resilience
entries: The worg is resistant to exhaustion and to the frightened condition.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) piercing damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a DC 13 STR save or be knocked prone.

bonus.name: Trail of Death (Recharge 4–6)
entries: The wraith moves up to 30 feet in a straight line. This distance is not reduced by moving through creatures or objects, and this movement doesn’t provoke opportunity attacks. The first time it enters a creature’s space during this move, that creature must succeed on a DC 15 DEX save or be afflicted with wasting doom. While afflicted with wasting doom, a creature takes 3 (1d6) necrotic damage at the start of each of its turns. Wasting doom ends if a creature starts its turn in sunlight or regains HP .

reaction.name: Unravel Life
entries: When a creature the wraith can see within 30 feet of it and that isn’t a Construct or Undead regains HP , the wraith can corrupt the healing energies, halving the amount of healing that creature receives. Wraith A flowing darkness hovers in the air, vaguely resembling a humanoid form in a tattered shroud. Only its eyes, two flickering points of hateful, red light, are clear.

reaction.name: Unlife Incarnate
entries: Of all undead, wraiths are the closest to the true antithesis to life. Little more than necrotic energy given form and the fractured sentience of a tortured soul, wraiths are typically hateful creatures that seek to snuff out any life, especially humanoid life, they encounter. A wraith’s touch drains the vitality of the living, and humanoids killed by a wraith return as spirits bound to its service.

reaction.name: Life Torn Asunder
entries: Life can’t survive long in the presence of a wraith. Plants wither as it passes, water dries along its path, and even the algae growing on a building shrivels ever so slightly as it slips through the wall. Animals flee before it, sensing its antithetical nature, and those who can’t flee fast enough leave bones in its wake. Only the light of the sun gives a wraith pause.

monster.name: Wraith
languages: the languages it knew in life

trait.name: Incorporeal Movement
entries: The wraith can move through other creatures and objects as if they were difficult terrain. It takes 5 (1d10) force damage if it ends its turn inside an object.

trait.name: Sunlight Sensitivity
entries: While in sunlight, the wraith has disadvantage on attack rolls, and its Perception is 7 when perceiving by sight.

trait.name: Undead Nature
entries: The wraith doesn’t require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Undead Resilience
entries: The wraith is immune to poison damage, to exhaustion, and to the poisoned condition.

action.name: Psychic Burst
entries: Melee or Ranged Spell Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 60 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (3d6 + 3) psychic damage.

bonus.name: Pronounce Fate
entries: The wyrdling pronounces the fate, either fortune or doom (the wyrdling’s choice), of one creature it can see within 30 feet of it. The target rolls a d4 and adds (fortune) or subtracts (doom) the number rolled to the next attack roll or save it makes before the start of the wyrdling’s next turn.

reaction.name: Call on Fate
entries: When the wyrdling is attacked by a creature it can see, it can pluck the strands of fate around the creature, imposing disadvantage on that attack roll. If the attack misses, the wyrdling or one friendly creature it can see within 30 feet of it has advantage on the next attack roll it makes against the attacker before the end of the wyrdling’s next turn. Wyrdling This dusky, hairless humanoid stands calmly, staring with eyes that seem to take in everything.

reaction.name: Children of Fate
entries: Wyrdlings were created long ago by a deity of fate. They form a mortal link between the world and destiny, acting as watchful eyes over the threads of fate and guiding those chosen by it. They spend much of their time living in isolated communities in remote areas, welcoming worthy travelers seeking their knowledge and vanishing like the mists to avoid the unworthy.

reaction.name: Identifying Tattoos
entries: Wyrdlings are slim and hairless, standing just shorter than most humans, with skin that ranges from a chalky gray to a smoky purple in color. Their pointed ears stand at angles from their heads. They have prominent canines, and their yellow eyes seem to glow with an inner light. Wyrdlings wear tattoos, typically on their heads, which signify to other wyrdlings the individual’s name, rank, and area of expertise with regards to fate. These tattoos can indicate that one wyrdling is a seeker of those fated to die violently while another counsels those fated to perform a great deed.

reaction.name: Privy to the Future
entries: Wyrdlings see patterns of fate as other humanoids see colors and hear sounds. In encounters with a wyrdling, it is not unusual for the wyrdling to know things without being told or to expect an encounter before it happens. However, much of what they see is out of context, and they may misinterpret information; wyrdlings aren’t omniscient and are prone to mistakes and misunderstanding as much as any other humanoid.

trait.name: Intuition
entries: The wyrdling rolls two dice for initiative, taking the higher number. In addition, the wyrdling has advantage on WIS (Insight) checks.

trait.name: Read Fate
entries: The wyrdling can spend 1 minute communing with fate on behalf of one creature that remains within 10 feet of it for the duration. The creature must ask a single question concerning a specific goal, event, or activity to occur within 7 days. At the end of the minute, the wyrdling pronounces the result of the target’s inquiry. This trait works like the Wyvern A great draconic beast wheels overhead, flexing the claws at the ends of its two legs and whipping a long, sinewy tail tipped with a wickedly curved stinger.

trait.name: Cousin of Dragons
entries: A distant cousin of true dragons, the wyvern is unique among its kin in that its forelimbs operate as both forelimbs and wings. Instead of a true dragon’s breath weapon, it has a venom-filled stinger on its tail, which it uses to quickly down its prey. A wyvern’s skin and scales tend towards colors that allow it to better blend in with the terrain of its environment: sandy browns and oranges in arid or desert climes, brows and deep blues in temperate mountains, and muddy browns and deep greens in heavily forested areas. Instinct-Driven Predator. Less intelligent than their draconic cousins, wyverns are still cunning hunters. They keep to the air while hunting, using the height and mobility of flight to slash with their claws and inject prey with the poison in their long, stinging tails. Without the additional forelimbs of its cousins, the wyvern is slower and clumsier when grounded and must use stealth and ambushes to catch prey. Wyverns are most active at dusk and dawn and primarily subsist on deer, elk, and similar prey.

trait.name: Clumsy
entries: While not flying, the wyvern is vulnerable to the grappled, prone, and restrained conditions.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The wyvern makes one Bite attack and one Stinger attack. While flying, it can replace one attack with a Claws attack.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) piercing damage.

action.name: Claws
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (2d8 + 4) slashing damage.

action.name: Stinger
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 15 ft., one creature. Hit: 15 (2d10 + 4) piercing damage plus 18 (4d8) poison damage. The target must succeed on a DC 15 CON save or be poisoned for 1 minute. A poisoned creature can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

reaction.name: Reflexive Tail Whip
entries: When a creature moves into a space within 15 feet of the wyvern, it can whip its tail at the creature. The target must succeed on a DC 15 STR save or be knocked prone. Xorn This broad, stony creature has a strange symmetry, with three arms, legs, and eyes evenly spaced around its barrel-like body. A massive, toothy maw opens atop its body.

reaction.name: Earthen Creatures
entries: Denizens of the Plane of Earth, the average xorn has a rotund body nearly as wide across as it is tall, supported on three stocky legs with broad, three-toed feet. Atop the body is a huge mouth full of teeth. Below the mouth are three large, round eyes, each positioned directly above a leg. Three powerful arms, placed equidistant between the eyes, emerge from the body, ending in clawed, three-fingered hands. Its entire body is rocky and commonly colored in earth tones, predominantly shades of brown or gray.

reaction.name: Earth Eaters
entries: Xorn move through the earth as if swimming through water. Though they can subsist on standard stone, raw gemstones, crystals, and precious metals are not only tasty to the xorn, but provide it with additional nutrients not found in standard stone. On their home plane, they flow through miles of stone each day, seeking these delicacies. When on the Material Plane or when their preferred raw meals are scarce, they settle for refined precious metals and gemstones, often putting them at odds with adventurers and merchants with pouches full of gold coins. Xorn are capable of speech, and when not starving, xorn are usually willing to negotiate or bargain with others for any gemstones or metals they might possess.

trait.name: Earth Glide
entries: The xorn can burrow through nonmagical, unworked earth and stone. While doing so, the xorn doesn’t disturb the material through which it moves.

trait.name: Stone Camouflage
entries: The xorn’s Stealth is 18 while in rocky terrain.

trait.name: Treasure Sense
entries: The xorn can pinpoint, by scent, the location of precious metals and stones, such as coins and gems, within 60 feet of it.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The xorn makes one Bite attack and three Claw attacks, or it makes three Lob Stone attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (3d6 + 3) piercing damage.

action.name: Claw
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (2d4 + 3) slashing damage.

action.name: Lob Stone
entries: Ranged Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, range 20/60 ft., one target. Hit: 12 (2d8 + 3) bludgeoning damage.

bonus.name: Shard Belch
entries: The xorn belches a mixture of stomach gas and crystalline shards in a 15-foot cone. Each creature in the area must succeed on a DC 14 DEX save or be coated in miniscule, crystalline shards until the end of its next turn. A creature coated in shards suffers bouts of pain each time it moves, reducing its speed by 10 feet and imposing disadvantage on all weapon attack rolls. Zombies A rotting corpse shambles forward, arms outstretched and flailing.

bonus.name: Animated Bodies
entries: Zombies are the animated corpses of the dead, brought to unlife by necromantic magic, creating tireless, fearless servants that obey without hesitation. Occasionally, zombies are produced by other methods. Victims of wights, for example, often rise as zombies under command of the undead that killed them. Sometimes an enduring source of necromantic magic corrupts an area, causing nearby corpses to spontaneously rise as zombies.

bonus.name: Decayed Corpses
entries: The appearance of zombies varies widely, depending upon the condition of the corpse prior to animation. A corpse dead from natural causes and stolen from an undertaker may be well-preserved, if obviously not alive, while a zombie animated from a drowning victim is bloated and sagging. Some necromancers use whatever is at hand. Others may choose the most disgusting corpses to add an additional fear factor for those that must face the zombies in combat.

bonus.name: Dim Intellect
entries: Zombies have no personality and little intellect, following only the most basic commands. They can wield weapons if armed but simply batter an opponent with its limbs if not. They do not have the intellect to avoid even obvious hazards, shambling into open pits if they exist between them and a target or walking heedlessly through a fire to attack, unless commanded otherwise in that moment. When not under specific orders, a zombie simply stands awaiting commands, though if a living creature comes close enough for it to detect, it attacks, as the dark magics animating a zombie make it malicious toward the living.

bonus.name: Non-Human Zombies
entries: Zombies are most commonly animated human remains, but the flesh of any living thing can be animated as a zombie. Zombie giants, for example, would be stronger and harder to kill than a human zombie. They would attack like other zombies, though they could throw rocks like a living giant if commanded to do so. Non- humanoid zombies typically attack as they did when living. A zombie dragon, for example, would attack with claws and teeth, and it could still fly if its wings were intact.

monster.name: Zombie
languages: understands the languages it knew in life but can’t speak

trait.name: Undead Fortitude
entries: If damage reduces the zombie to 0 HP , it must make a CON save with a DC equal to 5 + the damage taken, unless the damage is radiant or from a critical hit. On a success, the zombie drops to 1 HP instead.

trait.name: Undead Nature
entries: The zombie doesn’t require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Undead Resilience
entries: The zombie is immune to poison damage, to exhaustion, and to the poisoned condition.

action.name: Slam
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) bludgeoning damage. The target is grappled (escape DC 12) if it is a Medium or smaller creature, and the zombie doesn’t already have a creature grappled.

bonus.name: Rotten Hold
entries: The zombie gnaws idly on the creature grappled by it. The target must succeed on a DC 12 CON save or take 2 (1d4) poison damage. A Humanoid slain by this bonus action rises 24 hours later as a zombie, unless the Humanoid is restored to life or its body is destroyed.

bonus.name: Lumbering Charge (Recharge 5–6)
entries: The ogre zombie shoulders its weapon and charges forward, shoving into creatures on its way. It moves up to 20 feet in a straight line and can move through the space of any Medium or smaller creature. The first time it enters a creature’s space during this move, that creature must make a DC 14 STR save. On a failure, a creature takes 14 (4d6) bludgeoning damage and is knocked prone. On a success, a creature takes half the damage and isn’t knocked prone.

monster.name: Ogre Zombie
languages: understands Common and Giant but can’t speak

trait.name: Undead Fortitude
entries: If damage reduces the zombie to 0 HP , it must make a CON save with a DC equal to 5 + the damage taken, unless the damage is radiant or from a critical hit. On a success, the zombie drops to 1 HP instead.

trait.name: Undead Nature
entries: The zombie doesn’t require air, food, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Undead Resilience
entries: The zombie is immune to poison damage, to exhaustion, and to the poisoned condition.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The ogre zombie makes two Greatclub attacks.

action.name: Greatclub
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (2d8 + 4) bludgeoning damage. This appendix contains statistics for various beasts and vermin, many of which regularly find homes as beasts of burden or livestock to various Humanoids across the world or as loyal companions to adventurers.

action.name: Familiars
entries: Some of the creatures listed in this appendix can be called to serve a spellcaster via the create familiar spell. These creatures are: bat, cat, crab, frog (toad), hawk, lizard, octopus, owl, poisonous snake, fish (quipper), rat, raven, seahorse, spider, or weasel. In addition, this book features other familiars that might have specific requirements, such as only serving spellcasters, or might require permission from a GM before choosing them as familiars. The Additional Familiars table lists where these familiars can be found in this book. action.name: Multiattack
entries: The ape makes two Fist attacks. If both Fist attacks hit one Medium or smaller creature, the target is grappled (escape DC 13), and the ape can’t grapple another creature.

action.name: Fist
